This shrine is identified as the place in what was formerly the township of Susa where Susanoo chose to enshrine his spirit.<ref>{{cite web |title=History |url=http://english.susa-jinja.jp/information/24 |access-date=2020-03-30 |website=Susa Shrine Official Website}}</ref> The shrine was also known as Jūsansho Daimyōjin (十三所大明神) and Susa no Ōmiya (須佐大宮 'Great Shrine of Susa') during the medieval and early modern periods.<ref>{{cite web |title=第十八番 須佐神社 |url=https://shinbutsu.jp/shrines-temples/susajinja/ |access-date=2020-03-30 |website=出雲國神仏霊場を巡る旅 (Izumo-no-kuni shinbutsu reijo o meguru tabi) |publisher=社寺縁座の会 (Shaji Enza no Kai)}}</ref><ref name="Kamon">{{cite web |title=須佐(稲田)氏 (Susa (Inada)-shi) |url=http://www.harimaya.com/o_kamon1/syake/south/s_susa.html |access-date=2020-03-30 |website=家紋World – World of KAMON}}</ref> The shrine's priestly lineage, the Susa (or Inada) clan (須佐氏 / 稲田氏), were considered to be the descendants of Susanoo via his son Yashimashino-no-Mikoto (八島篠命, the ''Kojiki''<nowiki/>'s Yashimajinumi-no-Kami)<ref>{{cite book |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=rh3lGVy1zLMC&pg=PP347 |title=飯石郡誌 (Iishi-gun shi) |publisher=飯石郡役所 (Iishi-gun yakusho) |year=1918 |page=247 |language=ja}}</ref><ref name="Jinjashi">{{cite book |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=X7ut5gI2vQkC&pg=PP626 |title=大日本神社志 (Dai-Nippon jinja shi) |publisher=大日本敬神会本部 (Dai-Nippon Keishinkai Honbu) |year=1933 |page=342}}</ref> or Ōkuninushi.<ref name="Kamon" /> Besides Susanoo, his consort Kushinadahime and her parents Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi are also enshrined here as auxiliary deities.<ref name="Jinjashi" /><ref>{{cite web |title=Dedicated Kami (deities or Japanese gods) |url=http://english.susa-jinja.jp/information/7 |access-date=2020-03-30 |website=Suga Shrine Official Website}}</ref>
== History == The shrine was founded in 776<ref name="出雲観光カイト出雲観光協会公式ホームヘーシ-2022a">{{Cite web |title=須佐神社｜出雲観光ガイド【出雲観光協会公式ホームページ】 |url=https://www.izumo-kankou.gr.jp/684 |access-date=2022-06-27 |website=出雲観光ガイド【出雲観光協会公式ホームページ】 |language=ja}}</ref>
The priests of the shrine are said to be descendants of [[Ōkuninushi]].<ref>{{ill|Ken'ichi Tanigawa|lt=Ken'ichi Tanigawa|lt=Tanigawa Ken'ichi|de|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanigawa Ken'ichi|qq=}} 『日本の神々 神社と聖地 7 山陰』（新装復刊） 2000th year 白水社 ISBN 978-4-560-02507-9</ref>
It is mentioned in the [[Engishiki]].<ref name="wwwizumoshinwacom-2022a">{{Cite web |title=須佐神社{{!}}ヤマタノオロチ伝説とゆかりの地{{!}}出雲神話とゆかりの地{{!}}縁結びパワースポットと出雲神話{{!}}島根浪漫旅 |url=https://www.izumo-shinwa.com/spot_dtl_susa.html |access-date=2022-06-27 |website=www.izumo-shinwa.com}}</ref>
== Description == Susa-jinja is built in the [[taisha-zukuri]] style and, in addition to the ''[[honden]]'', hosts a number of smaller shrines. Susa-jinja also claims to have "Seven Wonders"<ref name="susainfo1"/> littered across its grounds including a 1300-year-old [[Shinboku|sacred tree]],<ref name="wwwizumoshinwacom-2022a" /> a cherry tree that casts no shadow, and a saltwater well.
==Ancestry of priests== {{Eight generations of Izumo}}

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Susa Shrine | native_name = 須佐神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = File:Susa-jinja-honden.jpg | coordinates = [https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?language=ja&pagename=%E9%A0%88%E4%BD%90%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E5%87%BA%E9%9B%B2%E5%B8%82)&params=35_14_04.8_N_132_44_13.0_E_type:landmark_region:JP-32&title=%E9%A0%88%E4%BD%90%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE 35 degrees 14 minutes 04.8 seconds north latitude 132 degrees 44 minutes 13.0 seconds east longitude] | location = 730 Susa, Sada-cho, Izumo City, Shimane Prefecture | deity = [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] [[Kushinadahime]] [[Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi]]
| festival = April 18 | municipality = [[:en:Izumo|Izumo]] | website = https://www.susa-jinja.jp/ | architecture_type = [[Taisha-zukuri]] | established = 776<ref name="出雲観光カイト出雲観光協会公式ホームヘーシ-2022a" /> }}
{{Nihongo|[[:en:Susa Shrine|Susa Shrine]]|須佐神社|Susa Jinja}} is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] in [[:en:Izumo|Izumo]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It is the 18th Shinto shrine in the Enza-no-kai Organization<ref>{{Cite web |title=Izumo Province 神仏霊場 |url=https://shinbutsu.jp/ |access-date=2023-04-10 |website=出雲地方の由緒ある20の神社・仏閣を巡る |language=en-US}}</ref> list of shrines and [[:en:Buddhist temples in Japan|temples]] considered important to the historical region of Izumo, which includes other major shrines in Shimane and [[:en:Tottori Prefecture|Tottori]] Prefectures including [[Izumo-taisha|Izumo-taisha]], [[Kumano shrine|Kumano-taisha]], and [[:en:Ōgamiyama Jinja|Ōgamiyama Jinja]].
The main deity is [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], and his wife, [[Kushinadahime|Inada-hime]], and [[Kushinadahime|Inada-hime's]] parents, [[Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|Ashimazuchi-no-mikoto]] and [[Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|Temazuchi-no-mikoto]], are also enshrined.{{Efn|It is said that the three deities were enshrined at the shrine site on the opposite side of the Susa River (currently on the grounds of Yukari-kan) until the [[:en:Tenbun|Tenbun]] period.}}<ref name="susainfo1">{{Cite web |title=Susa Jinja - Information{{!}}Dedicated Kami (deities or Japanese gods) |url=http://english.susa-jinja.jp/information/7 |access-date=2023-04-10 |website=english.susa-jinja.jp}}</ref>
The shrine is listed in the ''Izumo'' ''Fudoki'' as one of five shrines in Iishi District that were registered with the [[Department of Divinities]]. This shrine is identified as the place in what was formerly the township of Susa where Susanoo chose to enshrine his spirit.<ref>{{cite web |title=History |url=http://english.susa-jinja.jp/information/24 |access-date=2020-03-30 |website=Susa Shrine Official Website}}</ref> The shrine was also known as Jūsansho Daimyōjin (十三所大明神) and Susa no Ōmiya (須佐大宮 'Great Shrine of Susa') during the medieval and early modern periods.<ref>{{cite web |title=第十八番 須佐神社 |url=https://shinbutsu.jp/shrines-temples/susajinja/ |access-date=2020-03-30 |website=出雲國神仏霊場を巡る旅 (Izumo-no-kuni shinbutsu reijo o meguru tabi) |publisher=社寺縁座の会 (Shaji Enza no Kai)}}</ref><ref name="Kamon">{{cite web |title=須佐(稲田)氏 (Susa (Inada)-shi) |url=http://www.harimaya.com/o_kamon1/syake/south/s_susa.html |access-date=2020-03-30 |website=家紋World – World of KAMON}}</ref> The shrine's priestly lineage, the Susa (or Inada) clan (須佐氏 / 稲田氏), were considered to be the descendants of Susanoo via his son Yashimashino-no-Mikoto (八島篠命, the ''Kojiki''<nowiki/>'s Yashimajinumi-no-Kami)<ref>{{cite book |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=rh3lGVy1zLMC&pg=PP347 |title=飯石郡誌 (Iishi-gun shi) |publisher=飯石郡役所 (Iishi-gun yakusho) |year=1918 |page=247 |language=ja}}</ref><ref name="Jinjashi">{{cite book |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=X7ut5gI2vQkC&pg=PP626 |title=大日本神社志 (Dai-Nippon jinja shi) |publisher=大日本敬神会本部 (Dai-Nippon Keishinkai Honbu) |year=1933 |page=342}}</ref> or Ōkuninushi.<ref name="Kamon" /> Besides Susanoo, his consort Kushinadahime and her parents Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi are also enshrined here as auxiliary deities.<ref name="Jinjashi" /><ref>{{cite web |title=Dedicated Kami (deities or Japanese gods) |url=http://english.susa-jinja.jp/information/7 |access-date=2020-03-30 |website=Suga Shrine Official Website}}</ref>
== History == The shrine was founded in 776<ref name="出雲観光カイト出雲観光協会公式ホームヘーシ-2022a">{{Cite web |title=須佐神社｜出雲観光ガイド【出雲観光協会公式ホームページ】 |url=https://www.izumo-kankou.gr.jp/684 |access-date=2022-06-27 |website=出雲観光ガイド【出雲観光協会公式ホームページ】 |language=ja}}</ref>
The priests of the shrine are said to be descendants of [[Ōkuninushi]].<ref>{{ill|Ken'ichi Tanigawa|lt=Ken'ichi Tanigawa|lt=Tanigawa Ken'ichi|de|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanigawa Ken'ichi|qq=}} 『日本の神々 神社と聖地 7 山陰』（新装復刊） 2000th year 白水社 ISBN 978-4-560-02507-9</ref>
It is mentioned in the [[Engishiki]].<ref name="wwwizumoshinwacom-2022a">{{Cite web |title=須佐神社{{!}}ヤマタノオロチ伝説とゆかりの地{{!}}出雲神話とゆかりの地{{!}}縁結びパワースポットと出雲神話{{!}}島根浪漫旅 |url=https://www.izumo-shinwa.com/spot_dtl_susa.html |access-date=2022-06-27 |website=www.izumo-shinwa.com}}</ref>
== Description == Susa-jinja is built in the [[taisha-zukuri]] style and, in addition to the ''[[honden]]'', hosts a number of smaller shrines. Susa-jinja also claims to have "Seven Wonders"<ref name="susainfo1"/> littered across its grounds including a 1300-year-old [[Shinboku|sacred tree]],<ref name="wwwizumoshinwacom-2022a" /> a cherry tree that casts no shadow, and a saltwater well.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Susa-jinja (Izumo)]][[en:Susa Shrine]][[ja:須佐神社 (出雲市)]][[sv:Susa-jinja]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:29, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:36, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Shinto Shrine}}
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Susa Shrine | native_name = 須佐神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = File:Susa-jinja-honden.jpg | coordinates = [https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?language=ja&pagename=%E9%A0%88%E4%BD%90%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E5%87%BA%E9%9B%B2%E5%B8%82)&params=35_14_04.8_N_132_44_13.0_E_type:landmark_region:JP-32&title=%E9%A0%88%E4%BD%90%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE 35 degrees 14 minutes 04.8 seconds north latitude 132 degrees 44 minutes 13.0 seconds east longitude] | location = 730 Susa, Sada-cho, Izumo City, Shimane Prefecture | deity = [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] [[Kushinadahime]] [[Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi]]
| festival = April 18 | municipality = [[:en:Izumo|Izumo]] | website = https://www.susa-jinja.jp/ | architecture_type = [[Taisha-zukuri]] | established = 776<ref name="出雲観光カイト出雲観光協会公式ホームヘーシ-2022a" /> }}
{{Nihongo|[[:en:Susa Shrine|Susa Shrine]]|須佐神社|Susa Jinja}} is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] in [[:en:Izumo|Izumo]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It is the 18th Shinto shrine in the Enza-no-kai Organization<ref>{{Cite web |title=Izumo Province 神仏霊場 |url=https://shinbutsu.jp/ |access-date=2023-04-10 |website=出雲地方の由緒ある20の神社・仏閣を巡る |language=en-US}}</ref> list of shrines and [[:en:Buddhist temples in Japan|temples]] considered important to the historical region of Izumo, which includes other major shrines in Shimane and [[:en:Tottori Prefecture|Tottori]] Prefectures including [[Izumo-taisha|Izumo-taisha]], [[Kumano shrine|Kumano-taisha]], and [[:en:Ōgamiyama Jinja|Ōgamiyama Jinja]].
The main deity is [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], and his wife, [[Kushinadahime|Inada-hime]], and [[Kushinadahime|Inada-hime's]] parents, [[Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|Ashimazuchi-no-mikoto]] and [[Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|Temazuchi-no-mikoto]], are also enshrined.{{Efn|It is said that the three deities were enshrined at the shrine site on the opposite side of the Susa River (currently on the grounds of Yukari-kan) until the [[:en:Tenbun|Tenbun]] period.}}<ref name="susainfo1">{{Cite web |title=Susa Jinja - Information{{!}}Dedicated Kami (deities or Japanese gods) |url=http://english.susa-jinja.jp/information/7 |access-date=2023-04-10 |website=english.susa-jinja.jp}}</ref>
The shrine is listed in the ''Izumo'' ''Fudoki'' as one of five shrines in Iishi District that were registered with the [[Department of Divinities]]. This shrine is identified as the place in what was formerly the township of Susa where Susanoo chose to enshrine his spirit.<ref>{{cite web |title=History |url=http://english.susa-jinja.jp/information/24 |access-date=2020-03-30 |website=Susa Shrine Official Website}}</ref> The shrine was also known as Jūsansho Daimyōjin (十三所大明神) and Susa no Ōmiya (須佐大宮 'Great Shrine of Susa') during the medieval and early modern periods.<ref>{{cite web |title=第十八番 須佐神社 |url=https://shinbutsu.jp/shrines-temples/susajinja/ |access-date=2020-03-30 |website=出雲國神仏霊場を巡る旅 (Izumo-no-kuni shinbutsu reijo o meguru tabi) |publisher=社寺縁座の会 (Shaji Enza no Kai)}}</ref><ref name="Kamon">{{cite web |title=須佐(稲田)氏 (Susa (Inada)-shi) |url=http://www.harimaya.com/o_kamon1/syake/south/s_susa.html |access-date=2020-03-30 |website=家紋World – World of KAMON}}</ref> The shrine's priestly lineage, the Susa (or Inada) clan (須佐氏 / 稲田氏), were considered to be the descendants of Susanoo via his son Yashimashino-no-Mikoto (八島篠命, the ''Kojiki''<nowiki/>'s Yashimajinumi-no-Kami)<ref>{{cite book |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=rh3lGVy1zLMC&pg=PP347 |title=飯石郡誌 (Iishi-gun shi) |publisher=飯石郡役所 (Iishi-gun yakusho) |year=1918 |page=247 |language=ja}}</ref><ref name="Jinjashi">{{cite book |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=X7ut5gI2vQkC&pg=PP626 |title=大日本神社志 (Dai-Nippon jinja shi) |publisher=大日本敬神会本部 (Dai-Nippon Keishinkai Honbu) |year=1933 |page=342}}</ref> or Ōkuninushi.<ref name="Kamon" /> Besides Susanoo, his consort Kushinadahime and her parents Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi are also enshrined here as auxiliary deities.<ref name="Jinjashi" /><ref>{{cite web |title=Dedicated Kami (deities or Japanese gods) |url=http://english.susa-jinja.jp/information/7 |access-date=2020-03-30 |website=Suga Shrine Official Website}}</ref>
== History == The shrine was founded in 776<ref name="出雲観光カイト出雲観光協会公式ホームヘーシ-2022a">{{Cite web |title=須佐神社｜出雲観光ガイド【出雲観光協会公式ホームページ】 |url=https://www.izumo-kankou.gr.jp/684 |access-date=2022-06-27 |website=出雲観光ガイド【出雲観光協会公式ホームページ】 |language=ja}}</ref>
The priests of the shrine are said to be descendants of [[Ōkuninushi]].<ref>{{ill|Ken'ichi Tanigawa|lt=Ken'ichi Tanigawa|lt=Tanigawa Ken'ichi|de|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanigawa Ken'ichi|qq=}} 『日本の神々 神社と聖地 7 山陰』（新装復刊） 2000th year 白水社 ISBN 978-4-560-02507-9</ref>
It is mentioned in the [[Engishiki]].<ref name="wwwizumoshinwacom-2022a">{{Cite web |title=須佐神社{{!}}ヤマタノオロチ伝説とゆかりの地{{!}}出雲神話とゆかりの地{{!}}縁結びパワースポットと出雲神話{{!}}島根浪漫旅 |url=https://www.izumo-shinwa.com/spot_dtl_susa.html |access-date=2022-06-27 |website=www.izumo-shinwa.com}}</ref>
== Description == Susa-jinja is built in the [[taisha-zukuri]] style and, in addition to the ''[[honden]]'', hosts a number of smaller shrines. Susa-jinja also claims to have "Seven Wonders"<ref name="susainfo1"/> littered across its grounds including a 1300-year-old [[Shinboku|sacred tree]],<ref name="wwwizumoshinwacom-2022a" /> a cherry tree that casts no shadow, and a saltwater well.
==Ancestry of priests== {{Eight generations of Izumo}}

== History == The shrine was founded in 776<ref name="出雲観光カイト出雲観光協会公式ホームヘーシ-2022a">{{Cite web |title=須佐神社｜出雲観光ガイド【出雲観光協会公式ホームページ】 |url=https://www.izumo-kankou.gr.jp/684 |access-date=2022-06-27 |website=出雲観光ガイド【出雲観光協会公式ホームページ】 |language=ja}}</ref>
The priests of the shrine are said to be descendants of [[Ōkuninushi]].<ref>{{ill|Ken'ichi Tanigawa|lt=Ken'ichi Tanigawa|lt=Tanigawa Ken'ichi|de|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanigawa Ken'ichi|qq=}} 『日本の神々 神社と聖地 7 山陰』（新装復刊） 2000th year 白水社 ISBN 978-4-560-02507-9</ref>
It is mentioned in the [[Engishiki]].<ref name="wwwizumoshinwacom-2022a">{{Cite web |title=須佐神社{{!}}ヤマタノオロチ伝説とゆかりの地{{!}}出雲神話とゆかりの地{{!}}縁結びパワースポットと出雲神話{{!}}島根浪漫旅 |url=https://www.izumo-shinwa.com/spot_dtl_susa.html |access-date=2022-06-27 |website=www.izumo-shinwa.com}}</ref>
== Description == Susa-jinja is built in the [[taisha-zukuri]] style and, in addition to the ''[[honden]]'', hosts a number of smaller shrines. Susa-jinja also claims to have "Seven Wonders"<ref name="susainfo1"/> littered across its grounds including a 1300-year-old [[Shinboku|sacred tree]],<ref name="wwwizumoshinwacom-2022a" /> a cherry tree that casts no shadow, and a saltwater well.
==Ancestry of priests== {{Eight generations of Izumo}}

| festival = April 18 | municipality = [[:en:Izumo|Izumo]] | website = https://www.susa-jinja.jp/ | architecture_type = [[Taisha-zukuri]] | established = 776<ref name="出雲観光カイト出雲観光協会公式ホームヘーシ-2022a" /> }}
{{Nihongo|[[:en:Susa Shrine|Susa Shrine]]|須佐神社|Susa Jinja}} is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] in [[:en:Izumo|Izumo]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It is the 18th Shinto shrine in the Enza-no-kai Organization<ref>{{Cite web |title=Izumo Province 神仏霊場 |url=https://shinbutsu.jp/ |access-date=2023-04-10 |website=出雲地方の由緒ある20の神社・仏閣を巡る |language=en-US}}</ref> list of shrines and [[:en:Buddhist temples in Japan|temples]] considered important to the historical region of Izumo, which includes other major shrines in Shimane and [[:en:Tottori Prefecture|Tottori]] Prefectures including [[Izumo-taisha|Izumo-taisha]], [[Kumano shrine|Kumano-taisha]], and [[:en:Ōgamiyama Jinja|Ōgamiyama Jinja]].
The main deity is [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], and his wife, [[Kushinadahime|Inada-hime]], and [[Kushinadahime|Inada-hime's]] parents, [[Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|Ashimazuchi-no-mikoto]] and [[Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|Temazuchi-no-mikoto]], are also enshrined.{{Efn|It is said that the three deities were enshrined at the shrine site on the opposite side of the Susa River (currently on the grounds of Yukari-kan) until the [[:en:Tenbun|Tenbun]] period.}}<ref name="susainfo1">{{Cite web |title=Susa Jinja - Information{{!}}Dedicated Kami (deities or Japanese gods) |url=http://english.susa-jinja.jp/information/7 |access-date=2023-04-10 |website=english.susa-jinja.jp}}</ref>
The shrine is listed in the ''Izumo'' ''Fudoki'' as one of five shrines in Iishi District that were registered with the [[Department of Divinities]]. This shrine is identified as the place in what was formerly the township of Susa where Susanoo chose to enshrine his spirit.<ref>{{cite web |title=History |url=http://english.susa-jinja.jp/information/24 |access-date=2020-03-30 |website=Susa Shrine Official Website}}</ref> The shrine was also known as Jūsansho Daimyōjin (十三所大明神) and Susa no Ōmiya (須佐大宮 'Great Shrine of Susa') during the medieval and early modern periods.<ref>{{cite web |title=第十八番 須佐神社 |url=https://shinbutsu.jp/shrines-temples/susajinja/ |access-date=2020-03-30 |website=出雲國神仏霊場を巡る旅 (Izumo-no-kuni shinbutsu reijo o meguru tabi) |publisher=社寺縁座の会 (Shaji Enza no Kai)}}</ref><ref name="Kamon">{{cite web |title=須佐(稲田)氏 (Susa (Inada)-shi) |url=http://www.harimaya.com/o_kamon1/syake/south/s_susa.html |access-date=2020-03-30 |website=家紋World – World of KAMON}}</ref> The shrine's priestly lineage, the Susa (or Inada) clan (須佐氏 / 稲田氏), were considered to be the descendants of Susanoo via his son Yashimashino-no-Mikoto (八島篠命, the ''Kojiki''<nowiki/>'s Yashimajinumi-no-Kami)<ref>{{cite book |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=rh3lGVy1zLMC&pg=PP347 |title=飯石郡誌 (Iishi-gun shi) |publisher=飯石郡役所 (Iishi-gun yakusho) |year=1918 |page=247 |language=ja}}</ref><ref name="Jinjashi">{{cite book |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=X7ut5gI2vQkC&pg=PP626 |title=大日本神社志 (Dai-Nippon jinja shi) |publisher=大日本敬神会本部 (Dai-Nippon Keishinkai Honbu) |year=1933 |page=342}}</ref> or Ōkuninushi.<ref name="Kamon" /> Besides Susanoo, his consort Kushinadahime and her parents Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi are also enshrined here as auxiliary deities.<ref name="Jinjashi" /><ref>{{cite web |title=Dedicated Kami (deities or Japanese gods) |url=http://english.susa-jinja.jp/information/7 |access-date=2020-03-30 |website=Suga Shrine Official Website}}</ref>
== History == The shrine was founded in 776<ref name="出雲観光カイト出雲観光協会公式ホームヘーシ-2022a">{{Cite web |title=須佐神社｜出雲観光ガイド【出雲観光協会公式ホームページ】 |url=https://www.izumo-kankou.gr.jp/684 |access-date=2022-06-27 |website=出雲観光ガイド【出雲観光協会公式ホームページ】 |language=ja}}</ref>
The priests of the shrine are said to be descendants of [[Ōkuninushi]].<ref>{{ill|Ken'ichi Tanigawa|lt=Ken'ichi Tanigawa|lt=Tanigawa Ken'ichi|de|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanigawa Ken'ichi|qq=}} 『日本の神々 神社と聖地 7 山陰』（新装復刊） 2000th year 白水社 ISBN 978-4-560-02507-9</ref>
It is mentioned in the [[Engishiki]].<ref name="wwwizumoshinwacom-2022a">{{Cite web |title=須佐神社{{!}}ヤマタノオロチ伝説とゆかりの地{{!}}出雲神話とゆかりの地{{!}}縁結びパワースポットと出雲神話{{!}}島根浪漫旅 |url=https://www.izumo-shinwa.com/spot_dtl_susa.html |access-date=2022-06-27 |website=www.izumo-shinwa.com}}</ref>
== Description == Susa-jinja is built in the [[taisha-zukuri]] style and, in addition to the ''[[honden]]'', hosts a number of smaller shrines. Susa-jinja also claims to have "Seven Wonders"<ref name="susainfo1"/> littered across its grounds including a 1300-year-old [[Shinboku|sacred tree]],<ref name="wwwizumoshinwacom-2022a" /> a cherry tree that casts no shadow, and a saltwater well.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Susa-jinja (Izumo)]][[en:Susa Shrine]][[ja:須佐神社 (出雲市)]][[sv:Susa-jinja]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:29, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:36, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Shinto Shrine}}
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Susa Shrine | native_name = 須佐神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = File:Susa-jinja-honden.jpg | coordinates = [https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?language=ja&pagename=%E9%A0%88%E4%BD%90%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E5%87%BA%E9%9B%B2%E5%B8%82)&params=35_14_04.8_N_132_44_13.0_E_type:landmark_region:JP-32&title=%E9%A0%88%E4%BD%90%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE 35 degrees 14 minutes 04.8 seconds north latitude 132 degrees 44 minutes 13.0 seconds east longitude] | location = 730 Susa, Sada-cho, Izumo City, Shimane Prefecture | deity = [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] [[Kushinadahime]] [[Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi]]
| festival = April 18 | municipality = [[:en:Izumo|Izumo]] | website = https://www.susa-jinja.jp/ | architecture_type = [[Taisha-zukuri]] | established = 776<ref name="出雲観光カイト出雲観光協会公式ホームヘーシ-2022a" /> }}
{{Nihongo|[[:en:Susa Shrine|Susa Shrine]]|須佐神社|Susa Jinja}} is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] in [[:en:Izumo|Izumo]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It is the 18th Shinto shrine in the Enza-no-kai Organization<ref>{{Cite web |title=Izumo Province 神仏霊場 |url=https://shinbutsu.jp/ |access-date=2023-04-10 |website=出雲地方の由緒ある20の神社・仏閣を巡る |language=en-US}}</ref> list of shrines and [[:en:Buddhist temples in Japan|temples]] considered important to the historical region of Izumo, which includes other major shrines in Shimane and [[:en:Tottori Prefecture|Tottori]] Prefectures including [[Izumo-taisha|Izumo-taisha]], [[Kumano shrine|Kumano-taisha]], and [[:en:Ōgamiyama Jinja|Ōgamiyama Jinja]].
The main deity is [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], and his wife, [[Kushinadahime|Inada-hime]], and [[Kushinadahime|Inada-hime's]] parents, [[Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|Ashimazuchi-no-mikoto]] and [[Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|Temazuchi-no-mikoto]], are also enshrined.{{Efn|It is said that the three deities were enshrined at the shrine site on the opposite side of the Susa River (currently on the grounds of Yukari-kan) until the [[:en:Tenbun|Tenbun]] period.}}<ref name="susainfo1">{{Cite web |title=Susa Jinja - Information{{!}}Dedicated Kami (deities or Japanese gods) |url=http://english.susa-jinja.jp/information/7 |access-date=2023-04-10 |website=english.susa-jinja.jp}}</ref>
The shrine is listed in the ''Izumo'' ''Fudoki'' as one of five shrines in Iishi District that were registered with the [[Department of Divinities]]. This shrine is identified as the place in what was formerly the township of Susa where Susanoo chose to enshrine his spirit.<ref>{{cite web |title=History |url=http://english.susa-jinja.jp/information/24 |access-date=2020-03-30 |website=Susa Shrine Official Website}}</ref> The shrine was also known as Jūsansho Daimyōjin (十三所大明神) and Susa no Ōmiya (須佐大宮 'Great Shrine of Susa') during the medieval and early modern periods.<ref>{{cite web |title=第十八番 須佐神社 |url=https://shinbutsu.jp/shrines-temples/susajinja/ |access-date=2020-03-30 |website=出雲國神仏霊場を巡る旅 (Izumo-no-kuni shinbutsu reijo o meguru tabi) |publisher=社寺縁座の会 (Shaji Enza no Kai)}}</ref><ref name="Kamon">{{cite web |title=須佐(稲田)氏 (Susa (Inada)-shi) |url=http://www.harimaya.com/o_kamon1/syake/south/s_susa.html |access-date=2020-03-30 |website=家紋World – World of KAMON}}</ref> The shrine's priestly lineage, the Susa (or Inada) clan (須佐氏 / 稲田氏), were considered to be the descendants of Susanoo via his son Yashimashino-no-Mikoto (八島篠命, the ''Kojiki''<nowiki/>'s Yashimajinumi-no-Kami)<ref>{{cite book |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=rh3lGVy1zLMC&pg=PP347 |title=飯石郡誌 (Iishi-gun shi) |publisher=飯石郡役所 (Iishi-gun yakusho) |year=1918 |page=247 |language=ja}}</ref><ref name="Jinjashi">{{cite book |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=X7ut5gI2vQkC&pg=PP626 |title=大日本神社志 (Dai-Nippon jinja shi) |publisher=大日本敬神会本部 (Dai-Nippon Keishinkai Honbu) |year=1933 |page=342}}</ref> or Ōkuninushi.<ref name="Kamon" /> Besides Susanoo, his consort Kushinadahime and her parents Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi are also enshrined here as auxiliary deities.<ref name="Jinjashi" /><ref>{{cite web |title=Dedicated Kami (deities or Japanese gods) |url=http://english.susa-jinja.jp/information/7 |access-date=2020-03-30 |website=Suga Shrine Official Website}}</ref>
== History == The shrine was founded in 776<ref name="出雲観光カイト出雲観光協会公式ホームヘーシ-2022a">{{Cite web |title=須佐神社｜出雲観光ガイド【出雲観光協会公式ホームページ】 |url=https://www.izumo-kankou.gr.jp/684 |access-date=2022-06-27 |website=出雲観光ガイド【出雲観光協会公式ホームページ】 |language=ja}}</ref>
The priests of the shrine are said to be descendants of [[Ōkuninushi]].<ref>{{ill|Ken'ichi Tanigawa|lt=Ken'ichi Tanigawa|lt=Tanigawa Ken'ichi|de|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanigawa Ken'ichi|qq=}} 『日本の神々 神社と聖地 7 山陰』（新装復刊） 2000th year 白水社 ISBN 978-4-560-02507-9</ref>
It is mentioned in the [[Engishiki]].<ref name="wwwizumoshinwacom-2022a">{{Cite web |title=須佐神社{{!}}ヤマタノオロチ伝説とゆかりの地{{!}}出雲神話とゆかりの地{{!}}縁結びパワースポットと出雲神話{{!}}島根浪漫旅 |url=https://www.izumo-shinwa.com/spot_dtl_susa.html |access-date=2022-06-27 |website=www.izumo-shinwa.com}}</ref>
== Description == Susa-jinja is built in the [[taisha-zukuri]] style and, in addition to the ''[[honden]]'', hosts a number of smaller shrines. Susa-jinja also claims to have "Seven Wonders"<ref name="susainfo1"/> littered across its grounds including a 1300-year-old [[Shinboku|sacred tree]],<ref name="wwwizumoshinwacom-2022a" /> a cherry tree that casts no shadow, and a saltwater well.
==Ancestry of priests== {{Eight generations of Izumo}}

It is the 18th Shinto shrine in the Enza-no-kai Organization<ref>{{Cite web |title=Izumo Province 神仏霊場 |url=https://shinbutsu.jp/ |access-date=2023-04-10 |website=出雲地方の由緒ある20の神社・仏閣を巡る |language=en-US}}</ref> list of shrines and [[:en:Buddhist temples in Japan|temples]] considered important to the historical region of Izumo, which includes other major shrines in Shimane and [[:en:Tottori Prefecture|Tottori]] Prefectures including [[Izumo-taisha|Izumo-taisha]], [[Kumano shrine|Kumano-taisha]], and [[:en:Ōgamiyama Jinja|Ōgamiyama Jinja]].

This shrine is identified as the place in what was formerly the township of Susa where Susanoo chose to enshrine his spirit.<ref>{{cite web |title=History |url=http://english.susa-jinja.jp/information/24 |access-date=2020-03-30 |website=Susa Shrine Official Website}}</ref> The shrine was also known as Jūsansho Daimyōjin (十三所大明神) and Susa no Ōmiya (須佐大宮 'Great Shrine of Susa') during the medieval and early modern periods.<ref>{{cite web |title=第十八番 須佐神社 |url=https://shinbutsu.jp/shrines-temples/susajinja/ |access-date=2020-03-30 |website=出雲國神仏霊場を巡る旅 (Izumo-no-kuni shinbutsu reijo o meguru tabi) |publisher=社寺縁座の会 (Shaji Enza no Kai)}}</ref><ref name="Kamon">{{cite web |title=須佐(稲田)氏 (Susa (Inada)-shi) |url=http://www.harimaya.com/o_kamon1/syake/south/s_susa.html |access-date=2020-03-30 |website=家紋World – World of KAMON}}</ref> The shrine's priestly lineage, the Susa (or Inada) clan (須佐氏 / 稲田氏), were considered to be the descendants of Susanoo via his son Yashimashino-no-Mikoto (八島篠命, the ''Kojiki''<nowiki/>'s Yashimajinumi-no-Kami)<ref>{{cite book |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=rh3lGVy1zLMC&pg=PP347 |title=飯石郡誌 (Iishi-gun shi) |publisher=飯石郡役所 (Iishi-gun yakusho) |year=1918 |page=247 |language=ja}}</ref><ref name="Jinjashi">{{cite book |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=X7ut5gI2vQkC&pg=PP626 |title=大日本神社志 (Dai-Nippon jinja shi) |publisher=大日本敬神会本部 (Dai-Nippon Keishinkai Honbu) |year=1933 |page=342}}</ref> or Ōkuninushi.<ref name="Kamon" /> Besides Susanoo, his consort Kushinadahime and her parents Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi are also enshrined here as auxiliary deities.<ref name="Jinjashi" /><ref>{{cite web |title=Dedicated Kami (deities or Japanese gods) |url=http://english.susa-jinja.jp/information/7 |access-date=2020-03-30 |website=Suga Shrine Official Website}}</ref>
== History == The shrine was founded in 776<ref name="出雲観光カイト出雲観光協会公式ホームヘーシ-2022a">{{Cite web |title=須佐神社｜出雲観光ガイド【出雲観光協会公式ホームページ】 |url=https://www.izumo-kankou.gr.jp/684 |access-date=2022-06-27 |website=出雲観光ガイド【出雲観光協会公式ホームページ】 |language=ja}}</ref>

It is mentioned in the [[Engishiki]].<ref name="wwwizumoshinwacom-2022a">{{Cite web |title=須佐神社{{!}}ヤマタノオロチ伝説とゆかりの地{{!}}出雲神話とゆかりの地{{!}}縁結びパワースポットと出雲神話{{!}}島根浪漫旅 |url=https://www.izumo-shinwa.com/spot_dtl_susa.html |access-date=2022-06-27 |website=www.izumo-shinwa.com}}</ref>

Susa-jinja also claims to have "Seven Wonders"<ref name="susainfo1"/> littered across its grounds including a 1300-year-old [[Shinboku|sacred tree]],<ref name="wwwizumoshinwacom-2022a" /> a cherry tree that casts no shadow, and a saltwater well.

It is the 18th Shinto shrine in the Enza-no-kai Organization<ref>{{Cite web |title=Izumo Province 神仏霊場 |url=https://shinbutsu.jp/ |access-date=2023-04-10 |website=出雲地方の由緒ある20の神社・仏閣を巡る |language=en-US}}</ref> list of shrines and [[:en:Buddhist temples in Japan|temples]] considered important to the historical region of Izumo, which includes other major shrines in Shimane and [[:en:Tottori Prefecture|Tottori]] Prefectures including [[Izumo-taisha|Izumo-taisha]], [[Kumano shrine|Kumano-taisha]], and [[:en:Ōgamiyama Jinja|Ōgamiyama Jinja]].
The main deity is [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], and his wife, [[Kushinadahime|Inada-hime]], and [[Kushinadahime|Inada-hime's]] parents, [[Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|Ashimazuchi-no-mikoto]] and [[Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|Temazuchi-no-mikoto]], are also enshrined.{{Efn|It is said that the three deities were enshrined at the shrine site on the opposite side of the Susa River (currently on the grounds of Yukari-kan) until the [[:en:Tenbun|Tenbun]] period.}}<ref name="susainfo1">{{Cite web |title=Susa Jinja - Information{{!}}Dedicated Kami (deities or Japanese gods) |url=http://english.susa-jinja.jp/information/7 |access-date=2023-04-10 |website=english.susa-jinja.jp}}</ref>
The shrine is listed in the ''Izumo'' ''Fudoki'' as one of five shrines in Iishi District that were registered with the [[Department of Divinities]]. This shrine is identified as the place in what was formerly the township of Susa where Susanoo chose to enshrine his spirit.<ref>{{cite web |title=History |url=http://english.susa-jinja.jp/information/24 |access-date=2020-03-30 |website=Susa Shrine Official Website}}</ref> The shrine was also known as Jūsansho Daimyōjin (十三所大明神) and Susa no Ōmiya (須佐大宮 'Great Shrine of Susa') during the medieval and early modern periods.<ref>{{cite web |title=第十八番 須佐神社 |url=https://shinbutsu.jp/shrines-temples/susajinja/ |access-date=2020-03-30 |website=出雲國神仏霊場を巡る旅 (Izumo-no-kuni shinbutsu reijo o meguru tabi) |publisher=社寺縁座の会 (Shaji Enza no Kai)}}</ref><ref name="Kamon">{{cite web |title=須佐(稲田)氏 (Susa (Inada)-shi) |url=http://www.harimaya.com/o_kamon1/syake/south/s_susa.html |access-date=2020-03-30 |website=家紋World – World of KAMON}}</ref> The shrine's priestly lineage, the Susa (or Inada) clan (須佐氏 / 稲田氏), were considered to be the descendants of Susanoo via his son Yashimashino-no-Mikoto (八島篠命, the ''Kojiki''<nowiki/>'s Yashimajinumi-no-Kami)<ref>{{cite book |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=rh3lGVy1zLMC&pg=PP347 |title=飯石郡誌 (Iishi-gun shi) |publisher=飯石郡役所 (Iishi-gun yakusho) |year=1918 |page=247 |language=ja}}</ref><ref name="Jinjashi">{{cite book |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=X7ut5gI2vQkC&pg=PP626 |title=大日本神社志 (Dai-Nippon jinja shi) |publisher=大日本敬神会本部 (Dai-Nippon Keishinkai Honbu) |year=1933 |page=342}}</ref> or Ōkuninushi.<ref name="Kamon" /> Besides Susanoo, his consort Kushinadahime and her parents Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi are also enshrined here as auxiliary deities.<ref name="Jinjashi" /><ref>{{cite web |title=Dedicated Kami (deities or Japanese gods) |url=http://english.susa-jinja.jp/information/7 |access-date=2020-03-30 |website=Suga Shrine Official Website}}</ref>
== History == The shrine was founded in 776<ref name="出雲観光カイト出雲観光協会公式ホームヘーシ-2022a">{{Cite web |title=須佐神社｜出雲観光ガイド【出雲観光協会公式ホームページ】 |url=https://www.izumo-kankou.gr.jp/684 |access-date=2022-06-27 |website=出雲観光ガイド【出雲観光協会公式ホームページ】 |language=ja}}</ref>
The priests of the shrine are said to be descendants of [[Ōkuninushi]].<ref>{{ill|Ken'ichi Tanigawa|lt=Ken'ichi Tanigawa|lt=Tanigawa Ken'ichi|de|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanigawa Ken'ichi|qq=}} 『日本の神々 神社と聖地 7 山陰』（新装復刊） 2000th year 白水社 ISBN 978-4-560-02507-9</ref>
It is mentioned in the [[Engishiki]].<ref name="wwwizumoshinwacom-2022a">{{Cite web |title=須佐神社{{!}}ヤマタノオロチ伝説とゆかりの地{{!}}出雲神話とゆかりの地{{!}}縁結びパワースポットと出雲神話{{!}}島根浪漫旅 |url=https://www.izumo-shinwa.com/spot_dtl_susa.html |access-date=2022-06-27 |website=www.izumo-shinwa.com}}</ref>
== Description == Susa-jinja is built in the [[taisha-zukuri]] style and, in addition to the ''[[honden]]'', hosts a number of smaller shrines. Susa-jinja also claims to have "Seven Wonders"<ref name="susainfo1"/> littered across its grounds including a 1300-year-old [[Shinboku|sacred tree]],<ref name="wwwizumoshinwacom-2022a" /> a cherry tree that casts no shadow, and a saltwater well.
==Ancestry of priests== {{Eight generations of Izumo}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Susa-jinja (Izumo)]][[en:Susa Shrine]][[ja:須佐神社 (出雲市)]][[sv:Susa-jinja]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:29, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:36, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Shinto Shrine}}
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Susa Shrine | native_name = 須佐神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = File:Susa-jinja-honden.jpg | coordinates = [https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?language=ja&pagename=%E9%A0%88%E4%BD%90%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E5%87%BA%E9%9B%B2%E5%B8%82)&params=35_14_04.8_N_132_44_13.0_E_type:landmark_region:JP-32&title=%E9%A0%88%E4%BD%90%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE 35 degrees 14 minutes 04.8 seconds north latitude 132 degrees 44 minutes 13.0 seconds east longitude] | location = 730 Susa, Sada-cho, Izumo City, Shimane Prefecture | deity = [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] [[Kushinadahime]] [[Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi]]
| festival = April 18 | municipality = [[:en:Izumo|Izumo]] | website = https://www.susa-jinja.jp/ | architecture_type = [[Taisha-zukuri]] | established = 776<ref name="出雲観光カイト出雲観光協会公式ホームヘーシ-2022a" /> }}
{{Nihongo|[[:en:Susa Shrine|Susa Shrine]]|須佐神社|Susa Jinja}} is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] in [[:en:Izumo|Izumo]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It is the 18th Shinto shrine in the Enza-no-kai Organization<ref>{{Cite web |title=Izumo Province 神仏霊場 |url=https://shinbutsu.jp/ |access-date=2023-04-10 |website=出雲地方の由緒ある20の神社・仏閣を巡る |language=en-US}}</ref> list of shrines and [[:en:Buddhist temples in Japan|temples]] considered important to the historical region of Izumo, which includes other major shrines in Shimane and [[:en:Tottori Prefecture|Tottori]] Prefectures including [[Izumo-taisha|Izumo-taisha]], [[Kumano shrine|Kumano-taisha]], and [[:en:Ōgamiyama Jinja|Ōgamiyama Jinja]].
The main deity is [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], and his wife, [[Kushinadahime|Inada-hime]], and [[Kushinadahime|Inada-hime's]] parents, [[Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|Ashimazuchi-no-mikoto]] and [[Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|Temazuchi-no-mikoto]], are also enshrined.{{Efn|It is said that the three deities were enshrined at the shrine site on the opposite side of the Susa River (currently on the grounds of Yukari-kan) until the [[:en:Tenbun|Tenbun]] period.}}<ref name="susainfo1">{{Cite web |title=Susa Jinja - Information{{!}}Dedicated Kami (deities or Japanese gods) |url=http://english.susa-jinja.jp/information/7 |access-date=2023-04-10 |website=english.susa-jinja.jp}}</ref>
The shrine is listed in the ''Izumo'' ''Fudoki'' as one of five shrines in Iishi District that were registered with the [[Department of Divinities]]. This shrine is identified as the place in what was formerly the township of Susa where Susanoo chose to enshrine his spirit.<ref>{{cite web |title=History |url=http://english.susa-jinja.jp/information/24 |access-date=2020-03-30 |website=Susa Shrine Official Website}}</ref> The shrine was also known as Jūsansho Daimyōjin (十三所大明神) and Susa no Ōmiya (須佐大宮 'Great Shrine of Susa') during the medieval and early modern periods.<ref>{{cite web |title=第十八番 須佐神社 |url=https://shinbutsu.jp/shrines-temples/susajinja/ |access-date=2020-03-30 |website=出雲國神仏霊場を巡る旅 (Izumo-no-kuni shinbutsu reijo o meguru tabi) |publisher=社寺縁座の会 (Shaji Enza no Kai)}}</ref><ref name="Kamon">{{cite web |title=須佐(稲田)氏 (Susa (Inada)-shi) |url=http://www.harimaya.com/o_kamon1/syake/south/s_susa.html |access-date=2020-03-30 |website=家紋World – World of KAMON}}</ref> The shrine's priestly lineage, the Susa (or Inada) clan (須佐氏 / 稲田氏), were considered to be the descendants of Susanoo via his son Yashimashino-no-Mikoto (八島篠命, the ''Kojiki''<nowiki/>'s Yashimajinumi-no-Kami)<ref>{{cite book |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=rh3lGVy1zLMC&pg=PP347 |title=飯石郡誌 (Iishi-gun shi) |publisher=飯石郡役所 (Iishi-gun yakusho) |year=1918 |page=247 |language=ja}}</ref><ref name="Jinjashi">{{cite book |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=X7ut5gI2vQkC&pg=PP626 |title=大日本神社志 (Dai-Nippon jinja shi) |publisher=大日本敬神会本部 (Dai-Nippon Keishinkai Honbu) |year=1933 |page=342}}</ref> or Ōkuninushi.<ref name="Kamon" /> Besides Susanoo, his consort Kushinadahime and her parents Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi are also enshrined here as auxiliary deities.<ref name="Jinjashi" /><ref>{{cite web |title=Dedicated Kami (deities or Japanese gods) |url=http://english.susa-jinja.jp/information/7 |access-date=2020-03-30 |website=Suga Shrine Official Website}}</ref>
== History == The shrine was founded in 776<ref name="出雲観光カイト出雲観光協会公式ホームヘーシ-2022a">{{Cite web |title=須佐神社｜出雲観光ガイド【出雲観光協会公式ホームページ】 |url=https://www.izumo-kankou.gr.jp/684 |access-date=2022-06-27 |website=出雲観光ガイド【出雲観光協会公式ホームページ】 |language=ja}}</ref>
The priests of the shrine are said to be descendants of [[Ōkuninushi]].<ref>{{ill|Ken'ichi Tanigawa|lt=Ken'ichi Tanigawa|lt=Tanigawa Ken'ichi|de|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanigawa Ken'ichi|qq=}} 『日本の神々 神社と聖地 7 山陰』（新装復刊） 2000th year 白水社 ISBN 978-4-560-02507-9</ref>
It is mentioned in the [[Engishiki]].<ref name="wwwizumoshinwacom-2022a">{{Cite web |title=須佐神社{{!}}ヤマタノオロチ伝説とゆかりの地{{!}}出雲神話とゆかりの地{{!}}縁結びパワースポットと出雲神話{{!}}島根浪漫旅 |url=https://www.izumo-shinwa.com/spot_dtl_susa.html |access-date=2022-06-27 |website=www.izumo-shinwa.com}}</ref>
== Description == Susa-jinja is built in the [[taisha-zukuri]] style and, in addition to the ''[[honden]]'', hosts a number of smaller shrines. Susa-jinja also claims to have "Seven Wonders"<ref name="susainfo1"/> littered across its grounds including a 1300-year-old [[Shinboku|sacred tree]],<ref name="wwwizumoshinwacom-2022a" /> a cherry tree that casts no shadow, and a saltwater well.
==Ancestry of priests== {{Eight generations of Izumo}}

{{Nihongo|[[:en:Susa Shrine|Susa Shrine]]|須佐神社|Susa Jinja}} is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] in [[:en:Izumo|Izumo]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It is the 18th Shinto shrine in the Enza-no-kai Organization<ref>{{Cite web |title=Izumo Province 神仏霊場 |url=https://shinbutsu.jp/ |access-date=2023-04-10 |website=出雲地方の由緒ある20の神社・仏閣を巡る |language=en-US}}</ref> list of shrines and [[:en:Buddhist temples in Japan|temples]] considered important to the historical region of Izumo, which includes other major shrines in Shimane and [[:en:Tottori Prefecture|Tottori]] Prefectures including [[Izumo-taisha|Izumo-taisha]], [[Kumano shrine|Kumano-taisha]], and [[:en:Ōgamiyama Jinja|Ōgamiyama Jinja]].
The main deity is [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], and his wife, [[Kushinadahime|Inada-hime]], and [[Kushinadahime|Inada-hime's]] parents, [[Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|Ashimazuchi-no-mikoto]] and [[Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|Temazuchi-no-mikoto]], are also enshrined.{{Efn|It is said that the three deities were enshrined at the shrine site on the opposite side of the Susa River (currently on the grounds of Yukari-kan) until the [[:en:Tenbun|Tenbun]] period.}}<ref name="susainfo1">{{Cite web |title=Susa Jinja - Information{{!}}Dedicated Kami (deities or Japanese gods) |url=http://english.susa-jinja.jp/information/7 |access-date=2023-04-10 |website=english.susa-jinja.jp}}</ref>
The shrine is listed in the ''Izumo'' ''Fudoki'' as one of five shrines in Iishi District that were registered with the [[Department of Divinities]]. This shrine is identified as the place in what was formerly the township of Susa where Susanoo chose to enshrine his spirit.<ref>{{cite web |title=History |url=http://english.susa-jinja.jp/information/24 |access-date=2020-03-30 |website=Susa Shrine Official Website}}</ref> The shrine was also known as Jūsansho Daimyōjin (十三所大明神) and Susa no Ōmiya (須佐大宮 'Great Shrine of Susa') during the medieval and early modern periods.<ref>{{cite web |title=第十八番 須佐神社 |url=https://shinbutsu.jp/shrines-temples/susajinja/ |access-date=2020-03-30 |website=出雲國神仏霊場を巡る旅 (Izumo-no-kuni shinbutsu reijo o meguru tabi) |publisher=社寺縁座の会 (Shaji Enza no Kai)}}</ref><ref name="Kamon">{{cite web |title=須佐(稲田)氏 (Susa (Inada)-shi) |url=http://www.harimaya.com/o_kamon1/syake/south/s_susa.html |access-date=2020-03-30 |website=家紋World – World of KAMON}}</ref> The shrine's priestly lineage, the Susa (or Inada) clan (須佐氏 / 稲田氏), were considered to be the descendants of Susanoo via his son Yashimashino-no-Mikoto (八島篠命, the ''Kojiki''<nowiki/>'s Yashimajinumi-no-Kami)<ref>{{cite book |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=rh3lGVy1zLMC&pg=PP347 |title=飯石郡誌 (Iishi-gun shi) |publisher=飯石郡役所 (Iishi-gun yakusho) |year=1918 |page=247 |language=ja}}</ref><ref name="Jinjashi">{{cite book |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=X7ut5gI2vQkC&pg=PP626 |title=大日本神社志 (Dai-Nippon jinja shi) |publisher=大日本敬神会本部 (Dai-Nippon Keishinkai Honbu) |year=1933 |page=342}}</ref> or Ōkuninushi.<ref name="Kamon" /> Besides Susanoo, his consort Kushinadahime and her parents Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi are also enshrined here as auxiliary deities.<ref name="Jinjashi" /><ref>{{cite web |title=Dedicated Kami (deities or Japanese gods) |url=http://english.susa-jinja.jp/information/7 |access-date=2020-03-30 |website=Suga Shrine Official Website}}</ref>
== History == The shrine was founded in 776<ref name="出雲観光カイト出雲観光協会公式ホームヘーシ-2022a">{{Cite web |title=須佐神社｜出雲観光ガイド【出雲観光協会公式ホームページ】 |url=https://www.izumo-kankou.gr.jp/684 |access-date=2022-06-27 |website=出雲観光ガイド【出雲観光協会公式ホームページ】 |language=ja}}</ref>
The priests of the shrine are said to be descendants of [[Ōkuninushi]].<ref>{{ill|Ken'ichi Tanigawa|lt=Ken'ichi Tanigawa|lt=Tanigawa Ken'ichi|de|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanigawa Ken'ichi|qq=}} 『日本の神々 神社と聖地 7 山陰』（新装復刊） 2000th year 白水社 ISBN 978-4-560-02507-9</ref>
It is mentioned in the [[Engishiki]].<ref name="wwwizumoshinwacom-2022a">{{Cite web |title=須佐神社{{!}}ヤマタノオロチ伝説とゆかりの地{{!}}出雲神話とゆかりの地{{!}}縁結びパワースポットと出雲神話{{!}}島根浪漫旅 |url=https://www.izumo-shinwa.com/spot_dtl_susa.html |access-date=2022-06-27 |website=www.izumo-shinwa.com}}</ref>
== Description == Susa-jinja is built in the [[taisha-zukuri]] style and, in addition to the ''[[honden]]'', hosts a number of smaller shrines. Susa-jinja also claims to have "Seven Wonders"<ref name="susainfo1"/> littered across its grounds including a 1300-year-old [[Shinboku|sacred tree]],<ref name="wwwizumoshinwacom-2022a" /> a cherry tree that casts no shadow, and a saltwater well.
==Ancestry of priests== {{Eight generations of Izumo}}

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Susa Shrine | native_name = 須佐神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = File:Susa-jinja-honden.jpg | coordinates = [https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?language=ja&pagename=%E9%A0%88%E4%BD%90%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E5%87%BA%E9%9B%B2%E5%B8%82)&params=35_14_04.8_N_132_44_13.0_E_type:landmark_region:JP-32&title=%E9%A0%88%E4%BD%90%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE 35 degrees 14 minutes 04.8 seconds north latitude 132 degrees 44 minutes 13.0 seconds east longitude] | location = 730 Susa, Sada-cho, Izumo City, Shimane Prefecture | deity = [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] [[Kushinadahime]] [[Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi]]
| festival = April 18 | municipality = [[:en:Izumo|Izumo]] | website = https://www.susa-jinja.jp/ | architecture_type = [[Taisha-zukuri]] | established = 776<ref name="出雲観光カイト出雲観光協会公式ホームヘーシ-2022a" /> }}
{{Nihongo|[[:en:Susa Shrine|Susa Shrine]]|須佐神社|Susa Jinja}} is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] in [[:en:Izumo|Izumo]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It is the 18th Shinto shrine in the Enza-no-kai Organization<ref>{{Cite web |title=Izumo Province 神仏霊場 |url=https://shinbutsu.jp/ |access-date=2023-04-10 |website=出雲地方の由緒ある20の神社・仏閣を巡る |language=en-US}}</ref> list of shrines and [[:en:Buddhist temples in Japan|temples]] considered important to the historical region of Izumo, which includes other major shrines in Shimane and [[:en:Tottori Prefecture|Tottori]] Prefectures including [[Izumo-taisha|Izumo-taisha]], [[Kumano shrine|Kumano-taisha]], and [[:en:Ōgamiyama Jinja|Ōgamiyama Jinja]].
The main deity is [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], and his wife, [[Kushinadahime|Inada-hime]], and [[Kushinadahime|Inada-hime's]] parents, [[Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|Ashimazuchi-no-mikoto]] and [[Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|Temazuchi-no-mikoto]], are also enshrined.{{Efn|It is said that the three deities were enshrined at the shrine site on the opposite side of the Susa River (currently on the grounds of Yukari-kan) until the [[:en:Tenbun|Tenbun]] period.}}<ref name="susainfo1">{{Cite web |title=Susa Jinja - Information{{!}}Dedicated Kami (deities or Japanese gods) |url=http://english.susa-jinja.jp/information/7 |access-date=2023-04-10 |website=english.susa-jinja.jp}}</ref>
The shrine is listed in the ''Izumo'' ''Fudoki'' as one of five shrines in Iishi District that were registered with the [[Department of Divinities]]. This shrine is identified as the place in what was formerly the township of Susa where Susanoo chose to enshrine his spirit.<ref>{{cite web |title=History |url=http://english.susa-jinja.jp/information/24 |access-date=2020-03-30 |website=Susa Shrine Official Website}}</ref> The shrine was also known as Jūsansho Daimyōjin (十三所大明神) and Susa no Ōmiya (須佐大宮 'Great Shrine of Susa') during the medieval and early modern periods.<ref>{{cite web |title=第十八番 須佐神社 |url=https://shinbutsu.jp/shrines-temples/susajinja/ |access-date=2020-03-30 |website=出雲國神仏霊場を巡る旅 (Izumo-no-kuni shinbutsu reijo o meguru tabi) |publisher=社寺縁座の会 (Shaji Enza no Kai)}}</ref><ref name="Kamon">{{cite web |title=須佐(稲田)氏 (Susa (Inada)-shi) |url=http://www.harimaya.com/o_kamon1/syake/south/s_susa.html |access-date=2020-03-30 |website=家紋World – World of KAMON}}</ref> The shrine's priestly lineage, the Susa (or Inada) clan (須佐氏 / 稲田氏), were considered to be the descendants of Susanoo via his son Yashimashino-no-Mikoto (八島篠命, the ''Kojiki''<nowiki/>'s Yashimajinumi-no-Kami)<ref>{{cite book |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=rh3lGVy1zLMC&pg=PP347 |title=飯石郡誌 (Iishi-gun shi) |publisher=飯石郡役所 (Iishi-gun yakusho) |year=1918 |page=247 |language=ja}}</ref><ref name="Jinjashi">{{cite book |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=X7ut5gI2vQkC&pg=PP626 |title=大日本神社志 (Dai-Nippon jinja shi) |publisher=大日本敬神会本部 (Dai-Nippon Keishinkai Honbu) |year=1933 |page=342}}</ref> or Ōkuninushi.<ref name="Kamon" /> Besides Susanoo, his consort Kushinadahime and her parents Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi are also enshrined here as auxiliary deities.<ref name="Jinjashi" /><ref>{{cite web |title=Dedicated Kami (deities or Japanese gods) |url=http://english.susa-jinja.jp/information/7 |access-date=2020-03-30 |website=Suga Shrine Official Website}}</ref>
== History == The shrine was founded in 776<ref name="出雲観光カイト出雲観光協会公式ホームヘーシ-2022a">{{Cite web |title=須佐神社｜出雲観光ガイド【出雲観光協会公式ホームページ】 |url=https://www.izumo-kankou.gr.jp/684 |access-date=2022-06-27 |website=出雲観光ガイド【出雲観光協会公式ホームページ】 |language=ja}}</ref>
The priests of the shrine are said to be descendants of [[Ōkuninushi]].<ref>{{ill|Ken'ichi Tanigawa|lt=Ken'ichi Tanigawa|lt=Tanigawa Ken'ichi|de|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanigawa Ken'ichi|qq=}} 『日本の神々 神社と聖地 7 山陰』（新装復刊） 2000th year 白水社 ISBN 978-4-560-02507-9</ref>
It is mentioned in the [[Engishiki]].<ref name="wwwizumoshinwacom-2022a">{{Cite web |title=須佐神社{{!}}ヤマタノオロチ伝説とゆかりの地{{!}}出雲神話とゆかりの地{{!}}縁結びパワースポットと出雲神話{{!}}島根浪漫旅 |url=https://www.izumo-shinwa.com/spot_dtl_susa.html |access-date=2022-06-27 |website=www.izumo-shinwa.com}}</ref>
== Description == Susa-jinja is built in the [[taisha-zukuri]] style and, in addition to the ''[[honden]]'', hosts a number of smaller shrines. Susa-jinja also claims to have "Seven Wonders"<ref name="susainfo1"/> littered across its grounds including a 1300-year-old [[Shinboku|sacred tree]],<ref name="wwwizumoshinwacom-2022a" /> a cherry tree that casts no shadow, and a saltwater well.

{{Nihongo|[[:en:Susa Shrine|Susa Shrine]]|須佐神社|Susa Jinja}} is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] in [[:en:Izumo|Izumo]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It is the 18th Shinto shrine in the Enza-no-kai Organization<ref>{{Cite web |title=Izumo Province 神仏霊場 |url=https://shinbutsu.jp/ |access-date=2023-04-10 |website=出雲地方の由緒ある20の神社・仏閣を巡る |language=en-US}}</ref> list of shrines and [[:en:Buddhist temples in Japan|temples]] considered important to the historical region of Izumo, which includes other major shrines in Shimane and [[:en:Tottori Prefecture|Tottori]] Prefectures including [[Izumo-taisha|Izumo-taisha]], [[Kumano shrine|Kumano-taisha]], and [[:en:Ōgamiyama Jinja|Ōgamiyama Jinja]].
The main deity is [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], and his wife, [[Kushinadahime|Inada-hime]], and [[Kushinadahime|Inada-hime's]] parents, [[Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|Ashimazuchi-no-mikoto]] and [[Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|Temazuchi-no-mikoto]], are also enshrined.{{Efn|It is said that the three deities were enshrined at the shrine site on the opposite side of the Susa River (currently on the grounds of Yukari-kan) until the [[:en:Tenbun|Tenbun]] period.}}<ref name="susainfo1">{{Cite web |title=Susa Jinja - Information{{!}}Dedicated Kami (deities or Japanese gods) |url=http://english.susa-jinja.jp/information/7 |access-date=2023-04-10 |website=english.susa-jinja.jp}}</ref>
The shrine is listed in the ''Izumo'' ''Fudoki'' as one of five shrines in Iishi District that were registered with the [[Department of Divinities]]. This shrine is identified as the place in what was formerly the township of Susa where Susanoo chose to enshrine his spirit.<ref>{{cite web |title=History |url=http://english.susa-jinja.jp/information/24 |access-date=2020-03-30 |website=Susa Shrine Official Website}}</ref> The shrine was also known as Jūsansho Daimyōjin (十三所大明神) and Susa no Ōmiya (須佐大宮 'Great Shrine of Susa') during the medieval and early modern periods.<ref>{{cite web |title=第十八番 須佐神社 |url=https://shinbutsu.jp/shrines-temples/susajinja/ |access-date=2020-03-30 |website=出雲國神仏霊場を巡る旅 (Izumo-no-kuni shinbutsu reijo o meguru tabi) |publisher=社寺縁座の会 (Shaji Enza no Kai)}}</ref><ref name="Kamon">{{cite web |title=須佐(稲田)氏 (Susa (Inada)-shi) |url=http://www.harimaya.com/o_kamon1/syake/south/s_susa.html |access-date=2020-03-30 |website=家紋World – World of KAMON}}</ref> The shrine's priestly lineage, the Susa (or Inada) clan (須佐氏 / 稲田氏), were considered to be the descendants of Susanoo via his son Yashimashino-no-Mikoto (八島篠命, the ''Kojiki''<nowiki/>'s Yashimajinumi-no-Kami)<ref>{{cite book |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=rh3lGVy1zLMC&pg=PP347 |title=飯石郡誌 (Iishi-gun shi) |publisher=飯石郡役所 (Iishi-gun yakusho) |year=1918 |page=247 |language=ja}}</ref><ref name="Jinjashi">{{cite book |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=X7ut5gI2vQkC&pg=PP626 |title=大日本神社志 (Dai-Nippon jinja shi) |publisher=大日本敬神会本部 (Dai-Nippon Keishinkai Honbu) |year=1933 |page=342}}</ref> or Ōkuninushi.<ref name="Kamon" /> Besides Susanoo, his consort Kushinadahime and her parents Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi are also enshrined here as auxiliary deities.<ref name="Jinjashi" /><ref>{{cite web |title=Dedicated Kami (deities or Japanese gods) |url=http://english.susa-jinja.jp/information/7 |access-date=2020-03-30 |website=Suga Shrine Official Website}}</ref>
== History == The shrine was founded in 776<ref name="出雲観光カイト出雲観光協会公式ホームヘーシ-2022a">{{Cite web |title=須佐神社｜出雲観光ガイド【出雲観光協会公式ホームページ】 |url=https://www.izumo-kankou.gr.jp/684 |access-date=2022-06-27 |website=出雲観光ガイド【出雲観光協会公式ホームページ】 |language=ja}}</ref>
The priests of the shrine are said to be descendants of [[Ōkuninushi]].<ref>{{ill|Ken'ichi Tanigawa|lt=Ken'ichi Tanigawa|lt=Tanigawa Ken'ichi|de|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanigawa Ken'ichi|qq=}} 『日本の神々 神社と聖地 7 山陰』（新装復刊） 2000th year 白水社 ISBN 978-4-560-02507-9</ref>
It is mentioned in the [[Engishiki]].<ref name="wwwizumoshinwacom-2022a">{{Cite web |title=須佐神社{{!}}ヤマタノオロチ伝説とゆかりの地{{!}}出雲神話とゆかりの地{{!}}縁結びパワースポットと出雲神話{{!}}島根浪漫旅 |url=https://www.izumo-shinwa.com/spot_dtl_susa.html |access-date=2022-06-27 |website=www.izumo-shinwa.com}}</ref>
== Description == Susa-jinja is built in the [[taisha-zukuri]] style and, in addition to the ''[[honden]]'', hosts a number of smaller shrines. Susa-jinja also claims to have "Seven Wonders"<ref name="susainfo1"/> littered across its grounds including a 1300-year-old [[Shinboku|sacred tree]],<ref name="wwwizumoshinwacom-2022a" /> a cherry tree that casts no shadow, and a saltwater well.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Susa-jinja (Izumo)]][[en:Susa Shrine]][[ja:須佐神社 (出雲市)]][[sv:Susa-jinja]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:29, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:36, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Shinto Shrine}}
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Susa Shrine | native_name = 須佐神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = File:Susa-jinja-honden.jpg | coordinates = [https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?language=ja&pagename=%E9%A0%88%E4%BD%90%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E5%87%BA%E9%9B%B2%E5%B8%82)&params=35_14_04.8_N_132_44_13.0_E_type:landmark_region:JP-32&title=%E9%A0%88%E4%BD%90%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE 35 degrees 14 minutes 04.8 seconds north latitude 132 degrees 44 minutes 13.0 seconds east longitude] | location = 730 Susa, Sada-cho, Izumo City, Shimane Prefecture | deity = [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] [[Kushinadahime]] [[Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi]]
| festival = April 18 | municipality = [[:en:Izumo|Izumo]] | website = https://www.susa-jinja.jp/ | architecture_type = [[Taisha-zukuri]] | established = 776<ref name="出雲観光カイト出雲観光協会公式ホームヘーシ-2022a" /> }}
{{Nihongo|[[:en:Susa Shrine|Susa Shrine]]|須佐神社|Susa Jinja}} is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] in [[:en:Izumo|Izumo]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It is the 18th Shinto shrine in the Enza-no-kai Organization<ref>{{Cite web |title=Izumo Province 神仏霊場 |url=https://shinbutsu.jp/ |access-date=2023-04-10 |website=出雲地方の由緒ある20の神社・仏閣を巡る |language=en-US}}</ref> list of shrines and [[:en:Buddhist temples in Japan|temples]] considered important to the historical region of Izumo, which includes other major shrines in Shimane and [[:en:Tottori Prefecture|Tottori]] Prefectures including [[Izumo-taisha|Izumo-taisha]], [[Kumano shrine|Kumano-taisha]], and [[:en:Ōgamiyama Jinja|Ōgamiyama Jinja]].
The main deity is [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], and his wife, [[Kushinadahime|Inada-hime]], and [[Kushinadahime|Inada-hime's]] parents, [[Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|Ashimazuchi-no-mikoto]] and [[Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|Temazuchi-no-mikoto]], are also enshrined.{{Efn|It is said that the three deities were enshrined at the shrine site on the opposite side of the Susa River (currently on the grounds of Yukari-kan) until the [[:en:Tenbun|Tenbun]] period.}}<ref name="susainfo1">{{Cite web |title=Susa Jinja - Information{{!}}Dedicated Kami (deities or Japanese gods) |url=http://english.susa-jinja.jp/information/7 |access-date=2023-04-10 |website=english.susa-jinja.jp}}</ref>
The shrine is listed in the ''Izumo'' ''Fudoki'' as one of five shrines in Iishi District that were registered with the [[Department of Divinities]]. This shrine is identified as the place in what was formerly the township of Susa where Susanoo chose to enshrine his spirit.<ref>{{cite web |title=History |url=http://english.susa-jinja.jp/information/24 |access-date=2020-03-30 |website=Susa Shrine Official Website}}</ref> The shrine was also known as Jūsansho Daimyōjin (十三所大明神) and Susa no Ōmiya (須佐大宮 'Great Shrine of Susa') during the medieval and early modern periods.<ref>{{cite web |title=第十八番 須佐神社 |url=https://shinbutsu.jp/shrines-temples/susajinja/ |access-date=2020-03-30 |website=出雲國神仏霊場を巡る旅 (Izumo-no-kuni shinbutsu reijo o meguru tabi) |publisher=社寺縁座の会 (Shaji Enza no Kai)}}</ref><ref name="Kamon">{{cite web |title=須佐(稲田)氏 (Susa (Inada)-shi) |url=http://www.harimaya.com/o_kamon1/syake/south/s_susa.html |access-date=2020-03-30 |website=家紋World – World of KAMON}}</ref> The shrine's priestly lineage, the Susa (or Inada) clan (須佐氏 / 稲田氏), were considered to be the descendants of Susanoo via his son Yashimashino-no-Mikoto (八島篠命, the ''Kojiki''<nowiki/>'s Yashimajinumi-no-Kami)<ref>{{cite book |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=rh3lGVy1zLMC&pg=PP347 |title=飯石郡誌 (Iishi-gun shi) |publisher=飯石郡役所 (Iishi-gun yakusho) |year=1918 |page=247 |language=ja}}</ref><ref name="Jinjashi">{{cite book |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=X7ut5gI2vQkC&pg=PP626 |title=大日本神社志 (Dai-Nippon jinja shi) |publisher=大日本敬神会本部 (Dai-Nippon Keishinkai Honbu) |year=1933 |page=342}}</ref> or Ōkuninushi.<ref name="Kamon" /> Besides Susanoo, his consort Kushinadahime and her parents Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi are also enshrined here as auxiliary deities.<ref name="Jinjashi" /><ref>{{cite web |title=Dedicated Kami (deities or Japanese gods) |url=http://english.susa-jinja.jp/information/7 |access-date=2020-03-30 |website=Suga Shrine Official Website}}</ref>
== History == The shrine was founded in 776<ref name="出雲観光カイト出雲観光協会公式ホームヘーシ-2022a">{{Cite web |title=須佐神社｜出雲観光ガイド【出雲観光協会公式ホームページ】 |url=https://www.izumo-kankou.gr.jp/684 |access-date=2022-06-27 |website=出雲観光ガイド【出雲観光協会公式ホームページ】 |language=ja}}</ref>
The priests of the shrine are said to be descendants of [[Ōkuninushi]].<ref>{{ill|Ken'ichi Tanigawa|lt=Ken'ichi Tanigawa|lt=Tanigawa Ken'ichi|de|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanigawa Ken'ichi|qq=}} 『日本の神々 神社と聖地 7 山陰』（新装復刊） 2000th year 白水社 ISBN 978-4-560-02507-9</ref>
It is mentioned in the [[Engishiki]].<ref name="wwwizumoshinwacom-2022a">{{Cite web |title=須佐神社{{!}}ヤマタノオロチ伝説とゆかりの地{{!}}出雲神話とゆかりの地{{!}}縁結びパワースポットと出雲神話{{!}}島根浪漫旅 |url=https://www.izumo-shinwa.com/spot_dtl_susa.html |access-date=2022-06-27 |website=www.izumo-shinwa.com}}</ref>
== Description == Susa-jinja is built in the [[taisha-zukuri]] style and, in addition to the ''[[honden]]'', hosts a number of smaller shrines. Susa-jinja also claims to have "Seven Wonders"<ref name="susainfo1"/> littered across its grounds including a 1300-year-old [[Shinboku|sacred tree]],<ref name="wwwizumoshinwacom-2022a" /> a cherry tree that casts no shadow, and a saltwater well.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Susa-jinja (Izumo)]][[en:Susa Shrine]][[ja:須佐神社 (出雲市)]][[sv:Susa-jinja]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Susa-jinja (Izumo)]][[en:Susa Shrine]][[ja:須佐神社 (出雲市)]][[sv:Susa-jinja]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:29, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:36, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Shinto Shrine}}
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Susa Shrine | native_name = 須佐神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = File:Susa-jinja-honden.jpg | coordinates = [https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?language=ja&pagename=%E9%A0%88%E4%BD%90%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E5%87%BA%E9%9B%B2%E5%B8%82)&params=35_14_04.8_N_132_44_13.0_E_type:landmark_region:JP-32&title=%E9%A0%88%E4%BD%90%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE 35 degrees 14 minutes 04.8 seconds north latitude 132 degrees 44 minutes 13.0 seconds east longitude] | location = 730 Susa, Sada-cho, Izumo City, Shimane Prefecture | deity = [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] [[Kushinadahime]] [[Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi]]
| festival = April 18 | municipality = [[:en:Izumo|Izumo]] | website = https://www.susa-jinja.jp/ | architecture_type = [[Taisha-zukuri]] | established = 776<ref name="出雲観光カイト出雲観光協会公式ホームヘーシ-2022a" /> }}
{{Nihongo|[[:en:Susa Shrine|Susa Shrine]]|須佐神社|Susa Jinja}} is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] in [[:en:Izumo|Izumo]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It is the 18th Shinto shrine in the Enza-no-kai Organization<ref>{{Cite web |title=Izumo Province 神仏霊場 |url=https://shinbutsu.jp/ |access-date=2023-04-10 |website=出雲地方の由緒ある20の神社・仏閣を巡る |language=en-US}}</ref> list of shrines and [[:en:Buddhist temples in Japan|temples]] considered important to the historical region of Izumo, which includes other major shrines in Shimane and [[:en:Tottori Prefecture|Tottori]] Prefectures including [[Izumo-taisha|Izumo-taisha]], [[Kumano shrine|Kumano-taisha]], and [[:en:Ōgamiyama Jinja|Ōgamiyama Jinja]].
The main deity is [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], and his wife, [[Kushinadahime|Inada-hime]], and [[Kushinadahime|Inada-hime's]] parents, [[Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|Ashimazuchi-no-mikoto]] and [[Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|Temazuchi-no-mikoto]], are also enshrined.{{Efn|It is said that the three deities were enshrined at the shrine site on the opposite side of the Susa River (currently on the grounds of Yukari-kan) until the [[:en:Tenbun|Tenbun]] period.}}<ref name="susainfo1">{{Cite web |title=Susa Jinja - Information{{!}}Dedicated Kami (deities or Japanese gods) |url=http://english.susa-jinja.jp/information/7 |access-date=2023-04-10 |website=english.susa-jinja.jp}}</ref>
The shrine is listed in the ''Izumo'' ''Fudoki'' as one of five shrines in Iishi District that were registered with the [[Department of Divinities]]. This shrine is identified as the place in what was formerly the township of Susa where Susanoo chose to enshrine his spirit.<ref>{{cite web |title=History |url=http://english.susa-jinja.jp/information/24 |access-date=2020-03-30 |website=Susa Shrine Official Website}}</ref> The shrine was also known as Jūsansho Daimyōjin (十三所大明神) and Susa no Ōmiya (須佐大宮 'Great Shrine of Susa') during the medieval and early modern periods.<ref>{{cite web |title=第十八番 須佐神社 |url=https://shinbutsu.jp/shrines-temples/susajinja/ |access-date=2020-03-30 |website=出雲國神仏霊場を巡る旅 (Izumo-no-kuni shinbutsu reijo o meguru tabi) |publisher=社寺縁座の会 (Shaji Enza no Kai)}}</ref><ref name="Kamon">{{cite web |title=須佐(稲田)氏 (Susa (Inada)-shi) |url=http://www.harimaya.com/o_kamon1/syake/south/s_susa.html |access-date=2020-03-30 |website=家紋World – World of KAMON}}</ref> The shrine's priestly lineage, the Susa (or Inada) clan (須佐氏 / 稲田氏), were considered to be the descendants of Susanoo via his son Yashimashino-no-Mikoto (八島篠命, the ''Kojiki''<nowiki/>'s Yashimajinumi-no-Kami)<ref>{{cite book |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=rh3lGVy1zLMC&pg=PP347 |title=飯石郡誌 (Iishi-gun shi) |publisher=飯石郡役所 (Iishi-gun yakusho) |year=1918 |page=247 |language=ja}}</ref><ref name="Jinjashi">{{cite book |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=X7ut5gI2vQkC&pg=PP626 |title=大日本神社志 (Dai-Nippon jinja shi) |publisher=大日本敬神会本部 (Dai-Nippon Keishinkai Honbu) |year=1933 |page=342}}</ref> or Ōkuninushi.<ref name="Kamon" /> Besides Susanoo, his consort Kushinadahime and her parents Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi are also enshrined here as auxiliary deities.<ref name="Jinjashi" /><ref>{{cite web |title=Dedicated Kami (deities or Japanese gods) |url=http://english.susa-jinja.jp/information/7 |access-date=2020-03-30 |website=Suga Shrine Official Website}}</ref>
== History == The shrine was founded in 776<ref name="出雲観光カイト出雲観光協会公式ホームヘーシ-2022a">{{Cite web |title=須佐神社｜出雲観光ガイド【出雲観光協会公式ホームページ】 |url=https://www.izumo-kankou.gr.jp/684 |access-date=2022-06-27 |website=出雲観光ガイド【出雲観光協会公式ホームページ】 |language=ja}}</ref>
The priests of the shrine are said to be descendants of [[Ōkuninushi]].<ref>{{ill|Ken'ichi Tanigawa|lt=Ken'ichi Tanigawa|lt=Tanigawa Ken'ichi|de|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanigawa Ken'ichi|qq=}} 『日本の神々 神社と聖地 7 山陰』（新装復刊） 2000th year 白水社 ISBN 978-4-560-02507-9</ref>
It is mentioned in the [[Engishiki]].<ref name="wwwizumoshinwacom-2022a">{{Cite web |title=須佐神社{{!}}ヤマタノオロチ伝説とゆかりの地{{!}}出雲神話とゆかりの地{{!}}縁結びパワースポットと出雲神話{{!}}島根浪漫旅 |url=https://www.izumo-shinwa.com/spot_dtl_susa.html |access-date=2022-06-27 |website=www.izumo-shinwa.com}}</ref>
== Description == Susa-jinja is built in the [[taisha-zukuri]] style and, in addition to the ''[[honden]]'', hosts a number of smaller shrines. Susa-jinja also claims to have "Seven Wonders"<ref name="susainfo1"/> littered across its grounds including a 1300-year-old [[Shinboku|sacred tree]],<ref name="wwwizumoshinwacom-2022a" /> a cherry tree that casts no shadow, and a saltwater well.
==Ancestry of priests== {{Eight generations of Izumo}}

{{Nihongo|[[:en:Susa Shrine|Susa Shrine]]|須佐神社|Susa Jinja}} is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] in [[:en:Izumo|Izumo]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It is the 18th Shinto shrine in the Enza-no-kai Organization<ref>{{Cite web |title=Izumo Province 神仏霊場 |url=https://shinbutsu.jp/ |access-date=2023-04-10 |website=出雲地方の由緒ある20の神社・仏閣を巡る |language=en-US}}</ref> list of shrines and [[:en:Buddhist temples in Japan|temples]] considered important to the historical region of Izumo, which includes other major shrines in Shimane and [[:en:Tottori Prefecture|Tottori]] Prefectures including [[Izumo-taisha|Izumo-taisha]], [[Kumano shrine|Kumano-taisha]], and [[:en:Ōgamiyama Jinja|Ōgamiyama Jinja]].
The main deity is [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], and his wife, [[Kushinadahime|Inada-hime]], and [[Kushinadahime|Inada-hime's]] parents, [[Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|Ashimazuchi-no-mikoto]] and [[Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|Temazuchi-no-mikoto]], are also enshrined.{{Efn|It is said that the three deities were enshrined at the shrine site on the opposite side of the Susa River (currently on the grounds of Yukari-kan) until the [[:en:Tenbun|Tenbun]] period.}}<ref name="susainfo1">{{Cite web |title=Susa Jinja - Information{{!}}Dedicated Kami (deities or Japanese gods) |url=http://english.susa-jinja.jp/information/7 |access-date=2023-04-10 |website=english.susa-jinja.jp}}</ref>
The shrine is listed in the ''Izumo'' ''Fudoki'' as one of five shrines in Iishi District that were registered with the [[Department of Divinities]]. This shrine is identified as the place in what was formerly the township of Susa where Susanoo chose to enshrine his spirit.<ref>{{cite web |title=History |url=http://english.susa-jinja.jp/information/24 |access-date=2020-03-30 |website=Susa Shrine Official Website}}</ref> The shrine was also known as Jūsansho Daimyōjin (十三所大明神) and Susa no Ōmiya (須佐大宮 'Great Shrine of Susa') during the medieval and early modern periods.<ref>{{cite web |title=第十八番 須佐神社 |url=https://shinbutsu.jp/shrines-temples/susajinja/ |access-date=2020-03-30 |website=出雲國神仏霊場を巡る旅 (Izumo-no-kuni shinbutsu reijo o meguru tabi) |publisher=社寺縁座の会 (Shaji Enza no Kai)}}</ref><ref name="Kamon">{{cite web |title=須佐(稲田)氏 (Susa (Inada)-shi) |url=http://www.harimaya.com/o_kamon1/syake/south/s_susa.html |access-date=2020-03-30 |website=家紋World – World of KAMON}}</ref> The shrine's priestly lineage, the Susa (or Inada) clan (須佐氏 / 稲田氏), were considered to be the descendants of Susanoo via his son Yashimashino-no-Mikoto (八島篠命, the ''Kojiki''<nowiki/>'s Yashimajinumi-no-Kami)<ref>{{cite book |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=rh3lGVy1zLMC&pg=PP347 |title=飯石郡誌 (Iishi-gun shi) |publisher=飯石郡役所 (Iishi-gun yakusho) |year=1918 |page=247 |language=ja}}</ref><ref name="Jinjashi">{{cite book |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=X7ut5gI2vQkC&pg=PP626 |title=大日本神社志 (Dai-Nippon jinja shi) |publisher=大日本敬神会本部 (Dai-Nippon Keishinkai Honbu) |year=1933 |page=342}}</ref> or Ōkuninushi.<ref name="Kamon" /> Besides Susanoo, his consort Kushinadahime and her parents Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi are also enshrined here as auxiliary deities.<ref name="Jinjashi" /><ref>{{cite web |title=Dedicated Kami (deities or Japanese gods) |url=http://english.susa-jinja.jp/information/7 |access-date=2020-03-30 |website=Suga Shrine Official Website}}</ref>
== History == The shrine was founded in 776<ref name="出雲観光カイト出雲観光協会公式ホームヘーシ-2022a">{{Cite web |title=須佐神社｜出雲観光ガイド【出雲観光協会公式ホームページ】 |url=https://www.izumo-kankou.gr.jp/684 |access-date=2022-06-27 |website=出雲観光ガイド【出雲観光協会公式ホームページ】 |language=ja}}</ref>
The priests of the shrine are said to be descendants of [[Ōkuninushi]].<ref>{{ill|Ken'ichi Tanigawa|lt=Ken'ichi Tanigawa|lt=Tanigawa Ken'ichi|de|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanigawa Ken'ichi|qq=}} 『日本の神々 神社と聖地 7 山陰』（新装復刊） 2000th year 白水社 ISBN 978-4-560-02507-9</ref>
It is mentioned in the [[Engishiki]].<ref name="wwwizumoshinwacom-2022a">{{Cite web |title=須佐神社{{!}}ヤマタノオロチ伝説とゆかりの地{{!}}出雲神話とゆかりの地{{!}}縁結びパワースポットと出雲神話{{!}}島根浪漫旅 |url=https://www.izumo-shinwa.com/spot_dtl_susa.html |access-date=2022-06-27 |website=www.izumo-shinwa.com}}</ref>
== Description == Susa-jinja is built in the [[taisha-zukuri]] style and, in addition to the ''[[honden]]'', hosts a number of smaller shrines. Susa-jinja also claims to have "Seven Wonders"<ref name="susainfo1"/> littered across its grounds including a 1300-year-old [[Shinboku|sacred tree]],<ref name="wwwizumoshinwacom-2022a" /> a cherry tree that casts no shadow, and a saltwater well.
==Ancestry of priests== {{Eight generations of Izumo}}
== Notes ==

It is the 18th Shinto shrine in the Enza-no-kai Organization<ref>{{Cite web |title=Izumo Province 神仏霊場 |url=https://shinbutsu.jp/ |access-date=2023-04-10 |website=出雲地方の由緒ある20の神社・仏閣を巡る |language=en-US}}</ref> list of shrines and [[:en:Buddhist temples in Japan|temples]] considered important to the historical region of Izumo, which includes other major shrines in Shimane and [[:en:Tottori Prefecture|Tottori]] Prefectures including [[Izumo-taisha|Izumo-taisha]], [[Kumano shrine|Kumano-taisha]], and [[:en:Ōgamiyama Jinja|Ōgamiyama Jinja]].
The main deity is [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], and his wife, [[Kushinadahime|Inada-hime]], and [[Kushinadahime|Inada-hime's]] parents, [[Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|Ashimazuchi-no-mikoto]] and [[Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|Temazuchi-no-mikoto]], are also enshrined.{{Efn|It is said that the three deities were enshrined at the shrine site on the opposite side of the Susa River (currently on the grounds of Yukari-kan) until the [[:en:Tenbun|Tenbun]] period.}}<ref name="susainfo1">{{Cite web |title=Susa Jinja - Information{{!}}Dedicated Kami (deities or Japanese gods) |url=http://english.susa-jinja.jp/information/7 |access-date=2023-04-10 |website=english.susa-jinja.jp}}</ref>
The shrine is listed in the ''Izumo'' ''Fudoki'' as one of five shrines in Iishi District that were registered with the [[Department of Divinities]]. This shrine is identified as the place in what was formerly the township of Susa where Susanoo chose to enshrine his spirit.<ref>{{cite web |title=History |url=http://english.susa-jinja.jp/information/24 |access-date=2020-03-30 |website=Susa Shrine Official Website}}</ref> The shrine was also known as Jūsansho Daimyōjin (十三所大明神) and Susa no Ōmiya (須佐大宮 'Great Shrine of Susa') during the medieval and early modern periods.<ref>{{cite web |title=第十八番 須佐神社 |url=https://shinbutsu.jp/shrines-temples/susajinja/ |access-date=2020-03-30 |website=出雲國神仏霊場を巡る旅 (Izumo-no-kuni shinbutsu reijo o meguru tabi) |publisher=社寺縁座の会 (Shaji Enza no Kai)}}</ref><ref name="Kamon">{{cite web |title=須佐(稲田)氏 (Susa (Inada)-shi) |url=http://www.harimaya.com/o_kamon1/syake/south/s_susa.html |access-date=2020-03-30 |website=家紋World – World of KAMON}}</ref> The shrine's priestly lineage, the Susa (or Inada) clan (須佐氏 / 稲田氏), were considered to be the descendants of Susanoo via his son Yashimashino-no-Mikoto (八島篠命, the ''Kojiki''<nowiki/>'s Yashimajinumi-no-Kami)<ref>{{cite book |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=rh3lGVy1zLMC&pg=PP347 |title=飯石郡誌 (Iishi-gun shi) |publisher=飯石郡役所 (Iishi-gun yakusho) |year=1918 |page=247 |language=ja}}</ref><ref name="Jinjashi">{{cite book |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=X7ut5gI2vQkC&pg=PP626 |title=大日本神社志 (Dai-Nippon jinja shi) |publisher=大日本敬神会本部 (Dai-Nippon Keishinkai Honbu) |year=1933 |page=342}}</ref> or Ōkuninushi.<ref name="Kamon" /> Besides Susanoo, his consort Kushinadahime and her parents Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi are also enshrined here as auxiliary deities.<ref name="Jinjashi" /><ref>{{cite web |title=Dedicated Kami (deities or Japanese gods) |url=http://english.susa-jinja.jp/information/7 |access-date=2020-03-30 |website=Suga Shrine Official Website}}</ref>
== History == The shrine was founded in 776<ref name="出雲観光カイト出雲観光協会公式ホームヘーシ-2022a">{{Cite web |title=須佐神社｜出雲観光ガイド【出雲観光協会公式ホームページ】 |url=https://www.izumo-kankou.gr.jp/684 |access-date=2022-06-27 |website=出雲観光ガイド【出雲観光協会公式ホームページ】 |language=ja}}</ref>

It is mentioned in the [[Engishiki]].<ref name="wwwizumoshinwacom-2022a">{{Cite web |title=須佐神社{{!}}ヤマタノオロチ伝説とゆかりの地{{!}}出雲神話とゆかりの地{{!}}縁結びパワースポットと出雲神話{{!}}島根浪漫旅 |url=https://www.izumo-shinwa.com/spot_dtl_susa.html |access-date=2022-06-27 |website=www.izumo-shinwa.com}}</ref>

The main deity is [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], and his wife, [[Kushinadahime|Inada-hime]], and [[Kushinadahime|Inada-hime's]] parents, [[Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|Ashimazuchi-no-mikoto]] and [[Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi|Temazuchi-no-mikoto]], are also enshrined.{{Efn|It is said that the three deities were enshrined at the shrine site on the opposite side of the Susa River (currently on the grounds of Yukari-kan) until the [[:en:Tenbun|Tenbun]] period.}}<ref name="susainfo1">{{Cite web |title=Susa Jinja - Information{{!}}Dedicated Kami (deities or Japanese gods) |url=http://english.susa-jinja.jp/information/7 |access-date=2023-04-10 |website=english.susa-jinja.jp}}</ref>
The shrine is listed in the ''Izumo'' ''Fudoki'' as one of five shrines in Iishi District that were registered with the [[Department of Divinities]]. This shrine is identified as the place in what was formerly the township of Susa where Susanoo chose to enshrine his spirit.<ref>{{cite web |title=History |url=http://english.susa-jinja.jp/information/24 |access-date=2020-03-30 |website=Susa Shrine Official Website}}</ref> The shrine was also known as Jūsansho Daimyōjin (十三所大明神) and Susa no Ōmiya (須佐大宮 'Great Shrine of Susa') during the medieval and early modern periods.<ref>{{cite web |title=第十八番 須佐神社 |url=https://shinbutsu.jp/shrines-temples/susajinja/ |access-date=2020-03-30 |website=出雲國神仏霊場を巡る旅 (Izumo-no-kuni shinbutsu reijo o meguru tabi) |publisher=社寺縁座の会 (Shaji Enza no Kai)}}</ref><ref name="Kamon">{{cite web |title=須佐(稲田)氏 (Susa (Inada)-shi) |url=http://www.harimaya.com/o_kamon1/syake/south/s_susa.html |access-date=2020-03-30 |website=家紋World – World of KAMON}}</ref> The shrine's priestly lineage, the Susa (or Inada) clan (須佐氏 / 稲田氏), were considered to be the descendants of Susanoo via his son Yashimashino-no-Mikoto (八島篠命, the ''Kojiki''<nowiki/>'s Yashimajinumi-no-Kami)<ref>{{cite book |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=rh3lGVy1zLMC&pg=PP347 |title=飯石郡誌 (Iishi-gun shi) |publisher=飯石郡役所 (Iishi-gun yakusho) |year=1918 |page=247 |language=ja}}</ref><ref name="Jinjashi">{{cite book |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=X7ut5gI2vQkC&pg=PP626 |title=大日本神社志 (Dai-Nippon jinja shi) |publisher=大日本敬神会本部 (Dai-Nippon Keishinkai Honbu) |year=1933 |page=342}}</ref> or Ōkuninushi.<ref name="Kamon" /> Besides Susanoo, his consort Kushinadahime and her parents Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi are also enshrined here as auxiliary deities.<ref name="Jinjashi" /><ref>{{cite web |title=Dedicated Kami (deities or Japanese gods) |url=http://english.susa-jinja.jp/information/7 |access-date=2020-03-30 |website=Suga Shrine Official Website}}</ref>
== History == The shrine was founded in 776<ref name="出雲観光カイト出雲観光協会公式ホームヘーシ-2022a">{{Cite web |title=須佐神社｜出雲観光ガイド【出雲観光協会公式ホームページ】 |url=https://www.izumo-kankou.gr.jp/684 |access-date=2022-06-27 |website=出雲観光ガイド【出雲観光協会公式ホームページ】 |language=ja}}</ref>
The priests of the shrine are said to be descendants of [[Ōkuninushi]].<ref>{{ill|Ken'ichi Tanigawa|lt=Ken'ichi Tanigawa|lt=Tanigawa Ken'ichi|de|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanigawa Ken'ichi|qq=}} 『日本の神々 神社と聖地 7 山陰』（新装復刊） 2000th year 白水社 ISBN 978-4-560-02507-9</ref>
It is mentioned in the [[Engishiki]].<ref name="wwwizumoshinwacom-2022a">{{Cite web |title=須佐神社{{!}}ヤマタノオロチ伝説とゆかりの地{{!}}出雲神話とゆかりの地{{!}}縁結びパワースポットと出雲神話{{!}}島根浪漫旅 |url=https://www.izumo-shinwa.com/spot_dtl_susa.html |access-date=2022-06-27 |website=www.izumo-shinwa.com}}</ref>
== Description == Susa-jinja is built in the [[taisha-zukuri]] style and, in addition to the ''[[honden]]'', hosts a number of smaller shrines. Susa-jinja also claims to have "Seven Wonders"<ref name="susainfo1"/> littered across its grounds including a 1300-year-old [[Shinboku|sacred tree]],<ref name="wwwizumoshinwacom-2022a" /> a cherry tree that casts no shadow, and a saltwater well.
==Ancestry of priests== {{Eight generations of Izumo}}
