'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '島根県出雲市佐田町宮内730', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Susa-jinja (Izumo)}} [[EN:Susa Shrine]] [[JA:須佐神社 (出雲市)]] [[SV:Susa-jinja]] {{wikidata link|Q11664502}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine | Name=Susa Shrine | Image=[[File:Main Sanctuary of Susa-jinja 001.jpg|Susa Shrine Main Hall|270px]]
Main Hall | Location={{jalink|Shimane Prefecture|Q132751}}{{jalink|Izumo City|Q825166}}730 Susa, Sada Town | Location={{coord|35|14|4.9|N|132|44|12.7|E|region:JP-32_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deity={{jalink|Susanoo|Q272993|Susanoo no Mikoto}} | Shrine rank = {{jalink|Shikiuchi-sha}} (small)
Old {{jalink|Modern shrine rank system #government shrine|Q712534|Kokuhei-kosha}}
{{jalink|Beppyo Shrine|Q10898274}} | Main shrine={{jalink|Taisha-zukuri|Q387874}} | Annual festival={{jalink|April 18th|Q2521}} | Shinto ritual = Nenbutsu dance | Temples, etc.={{jalink|Izumo Kuni Shinto and Buddhist Sacred Sites|Q11395860}}No. 18 | Alias = Susa Omiya|Map={{Location map|Japan Shimane|label=Susa Shrine|lat_deg=35|lat_min=14|lat_sec=4.9|lon_deg=132|lon_min=44|lon_sec=12.7|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point| marker=religious-shinto|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=250|coord={{coord|35|14|4.9|N|132|44|12.7|E|}}}} |Foundation = Age of the Gods}} '''Susa Shrine'' (Susa Jinja) is a {{jalink|Shrine|Q845945}} located in {{jalink|Shimane Prefecture|Q132751}}{{jalink|Izumo City|Q825166}}. An old shrine that enshrines the soul of {{jalink|Susanoo|Q272993|Susanoo no Mikoto}}. {{jalink|Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|Shikinai-kosha}}, and the old {{jalink|shakaku|Q10444029}} is {{jalink|modern company rank system|Q712534|Kokuhei-kosha}}. {{jalink|Izumo Kuni Sacred Sites|Q11395860}}No. 18. === History === In ``{{jalink|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|Q11395858}}'', there is a description that Susanoo no Mikoto came to this area after cultivating various areas, did the final cultivation, and said, ``This is a good country, so I will name the land, not Iwaki,'' and named it ``Susa'' to soothe his soul. Since ancient times, it has been considered the main shrine of Susanoo no Mikoto. The Susa clan of {{jalink|shake|Q11588367}} is said to have descended from {{jalink|Kayanarumi|Q11294873|Kayana-ryu Mime}}, the son of {{jalink|Ōkuninushi}}. The former site of the shrine is said to have been on Mt. Miyao to the north of the shrine. The current shrine site is located almost in the center of the basin, and it is thought that it was already in this area during the Middle Ages. It is described as ``Sosa Shrine'' in ``Izumo no Kuni Fudoki.'' In the ``{{jalink|Engishiki Shinmecho|Q11064932}}'', it is listed as ``Susa Shrine'' and is listed as a small shrine. In the medieval period, it was called ``Jusansho Daimyojin'' and ``Omiya Daimyojin,'' and in the early modern period, it was called ``Susa Omiya.'' In {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}}4 ({{jalink|1871|Q7746}}), the name was changed to "Susa Shrine" as described in the Engishiki, and in Meiji 5 ({{jalink|1872| Q7753}}), it was promoted to a local shrine, the following year in the 6th year of Meiji ({{jalink|1873|Q7757}}), it was promoted to a prefectural shrine, and in the 33rd year of Meiji ({{jalink|1900|Q2034}}) to a national shrine. === Enshrined God === ; Main enshrined deity :* {{jalink|Susanoo|Q272993|Susanoo Mikoto}} ; Deities enshrinedIt is said that the three deities were enshrined at a shrine on the opposite bank of the Susa River (currently within the grounds of Yukari-kan) until the {{jalink|Tenbun (era name)|Q1192853|Tenbun}}. :* {{jalink|Kushinadahime|Q505469|Inadahime no Mikoto}} - Susanoo no Mikoto's wife :* {{jalink|Ashinazuchi Tenazuchi|Q643763|Ashimatsuchi no Mikoto}} - Inadahime no Mikoto's parent :* {{jalink|Ashinazuchi Tenazuchi|Q643763|Temazuchi no Mikoto}} - Inadahime no Mikoto's parent, the ancestor of the Susa family === Facilities === ; Main shrine, Heiden, Worship hall :: The main shrine is a cultural property designated by Shimane Prefecture and was built in 1861. |publisher=Shimane Prefecture Education Agency Cultural Properties Division}}. It is a {{jalink|taisha-zukuri|Q387874}} building with a width of 2 rooms (approximately 4 m) and a height of approximately 12 m, and there is a floor (kizahashi) that serves as the entrance to the 1 room on the right. {{Cite web|Japanese book|url=https://www.susa-jinja.jp/information/8 |title=Gohonden (designated as an important prefectural cultural property) |access-date=2022-07-13 |publisher=Susa Shrine Official}}. The one before that is described as 4 rooms in the Omiyako map written in {{jalink|Gen'o|Q1193925}} 2nd year ({{jalink|1320|Q5768}}). ; Kaguraden ; Shrine office ; salt well:: It is said that Susanoo-no-Mikoto himself drew salt and purified this land. It is said to follow the Sea of ​​Japan, and when the {{jalink|Sea of ​​Japan|Q27092}} is at high tide, tide flowers blow on the ground near the {{jalink|well|Q43483}}. Analysis revealed that it was a "{{jalink|sodium sulfate|Q211737|mirabilite}} containing {{jalink|chloride spring|Q11428612|salt spring}}" and was weakly {{jalink|base|Q11193|alkaline}}{{Cite web|Japanese book|url=https://www.susa-jinja.jp/seven_wonders/1st |title=Shionoi (Shionoi) |access-date=2022-07-13 |publisher=Susa Shrine Official}}. ; Osugi :: Located behind the main shrine, the tree is estimated to be about 1300 years old, with a trunk circumference of 7 meters and a height of 30 meters. Since {{jalink|Hiroyuki Ebara|Q11550701}}'s ``Shinto Travelogue'' was published, many people who were unscrupulous were peeling off the bark of this cedar tree and taking it home with them, so a fence has now been placed around the trunk. File:Torii at the Entrance of Susa-jinja 001.jpg|alt=|Torii at the Entrance of Susa-jinjaFile:Worship Hall of Susa-jinja 001.jpg|alt=|Worship Hall of Susa-jinja File:Heiden Hall (A Hall for Making Offerings to the God) of Susa-jinja 001.jpg|alt=|Heiden Hall File:Kagura Hall (Sacred Dance Hall) in Susa-jinja 001.jpg|alt=|Kagura Hall File:Sacred Saltwater Well in Susa-jinja 001.jpg|alt=|Salt Well File:Sacred Tree (Big Japanese Cedar) in Susa-jinja 001.jpg|alt=|Big Japanese Cedar === Precinct Company === [[File:Main Sanctuary of Auxiliary Amaterasu-jinja in Susa-jinja 001.jpg|thumb|Amaterasusha Main Hall]] ; Amaterasusha :: Deity enshrined: {{jalink|Amaterasu|Q455602}}. It is located across the road in front of the shrine. {{Cite web|Japanese book|url=https://www.susa-jinja.jp/information/25 |title=Susasha |access-date=2022-07-13 |publisher=Susa Shrine Official}}, which is also called "Kami-gozen-san" or "Kami-sha". In {{jalink|Middle Ages|Q12554}} it was called Ise Palace. ; Higashi/Nishisuesha :: Enshrined deity: {{jalink|Amenooshihomimi|Q462555|Amenohomi no Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Amenohohi|Q10940685|Amenohimi no Mikoto}} , {{jalink|Amatsu Hikone Mikoto|Q10940228|Amatsu Hikone Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Ikutsu Hikone Mikoto|Q11147583|Katsu Hikone Mikoto}} , {{jalink|Kumano Kusubi|Q11298796|Kumano Kushihime no Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|Ichikishima Hime no Mikoto} }, {{jalink|Takiribime|Q10932870|Tagitsuhime no Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Tagitsuhime|Q10932592|Tashinhime no Mikoto}}. ; Mihosha:: Deities enshrined: {{jalink|Mihotsuhime|Q6378698|Mihotsu Hime-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Kotoshiro-shu|Q8009611|Kotoshiro-shu-no-Mikoto}}. Also called "Shimo no Gozen-san" or "Shimosha." ; Inarisha :: Enshrined deity: {{jalink|Ukanomitama|Q3080728|Inakura Tama no Mikoto}}. ; Zuishinmon :: Deities enshrined: {{jalink|Amenshimonbetsukami|Q10940542|Toyowamatokami and Kushiwamatokami}}. File:Worship Hall of Auxiliary Amaterasu-jinja in Susa-jinja 001.jpg|alt=|Amaterasusha Worship Hall File:Auxiliary East-jinja in Susa-jinja 001.jpg|alt=|Tosuesha File:Auxiliary West-jinja in Susa-jinja 001.jpg|alt=|Nishisuesha File:Auxiliary Miho-jinja in Susa-jinja 001.jpg|alt=|Mihosha File:Auxiliary Inari-jinja in Susa-jinja 001.jpg|alt=|Inarisha File:Main Gate in Susa-jinja 001.jpg|alt=|Zuishinmon === Kyougaisha === ; Itsukushima Shrine :: Deities enshrined: Ichikishima Hime no Mikoto, Tagoko Hime no Mikoto, Tanatsu Hime no Mikoto ({{jalink|Three goddesses of Munakata|Q10948069}}). Also called ``Seidosha'' and ``Haridosha''. ; Suga Shrine :: Enshrined deity: Suho-no-son. is also called "Saikagura-san". === Festival === * January 1st {{jalink|Saidan Festival|Q11546824}} * February 3rd {{jalink|Setsubun|Q746798|Setsubun Festival}} ** {{jalink|Kagura|Q1348260}} dedication, {{jalink|Great Purification|Q11261910|Chino Ring}}, and bean-throwing will be held. *February 17th Prayer Festival * April 18th {{jalink|Regular Festival|Q11385469}} ** Asashi Matsuri - After the regular festival ritual, the festival is carried out from the main shrine to Amaterasu Shrine across the street. |access-date=2022-07-13 |publisher=Susa Shrine Official}}. * April 19 Koden Festival ** Ryoou-mai - Performed following the Koden Festival ritual. {{Cite web|Japanese book|url=https://www.susa-jinja.jp/event/27/29 |title=Ryoou no Mai |access-date=2022-07-13 |publisher=Susa Shrine Official}}. It is thought that it was originally danced by a divine being of the Onmyoji type, but was transformed into one.** Hundred-handed Shinji - Archery ritual held in the afternoon to pray for the exorcism of demons and a good harvest. * June 30th {{jalink|Great Purification|Q11261910}} * August 15 Kirimei Shinto ritual ** {{jalink|Nenbutsu Dance|Q11491438}} - Held in the afternoon. Two ritual flowers are erected in the shrine grounds, and beneath them dancers wearing kimono (kimono) form a circle, chanting ``Naamamido'' and dancing monotonously to the beat of the flute. It is a dance with strong {{jalink|Shinbutsu syncretism|Q2279088}} color, which is thought to have been influenced by Nembutsu saints in the {{jalink|Dengaku|Q3022455}} style of dance in the medieval period. * October 17th Autumn Festival * November 23rd Niinamatsuri * December 31st Purification * 15th of every month {{jalink|Monthly Festival|Q11516161}} === Cultural assets === === National Important Cultural Property === * Hyogo Kunishitei (unmarked contents) - {{jalink|1912|Q2077}} ({{jalink|Taisho|Q329174}} first year) Designated on September 3rd{{Cite web|Japanese book|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/6331 |title=Hyogo Kunishitei (unwritten contents) / National Treasures and Important Cultural Properties (Artworks) |access-date=2022-07-13 |publisher=Nationally Designated Cultural Properties Database / Agency for Cultural Affairs}}. {{jalink|Tenbun (era name)|Q1192853|Tenbun}} dedicated by {{jalink|Haruhisa Amago|Q3273106}} in 23 ({{jalink|1554|Q6570}}). === Designated by Shimane Prefecture === ; Tangible cultural property * (Building) Main Hall of Susa Shrine - Designated on May 31, {{jalink|1966|Q2649}} ({{jalink|Showa|Q327567}}41). * (Sculpture) Bugaku mask "Nosori" - {{jalink|1972|Q2476}} (Designated on July 28, 1972) |access-date=2022-07-13 |publisher=Shimane Prefecture Education Agency Cultural Properties Division}}. * (Craft) Kurowei Armor Remains - {{jalink|2005|Q2019}} ({{jalink|Heisei|Q234510}}17) Designated April 15th {{Cite web|Japanese book|url=https://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shimane/kougeihin.html |title=List of prefecture-designated cultural properties (crafts) / Cultural properties |access-date=2022-07-13 |publisher=Shimane Prefecture Education Agency Cultural Properties Division}}. ; Intangible folk cultural assets* Nenbutsu dance at Susa Shrine - {{jalink|1961|Q3696}} (Designated on June 13, 1966) |access-date=2022-07-13 |publisher=Shimane Prefecture Education Agency Cultural Properties Division}}. === Transportation === * From {{jalink|Izumo City Station|Q860288}}, take the {{jalink|Ichibata Bus|Q11352955}} bound for Izumo Susa and get off at Susa bus stop. 3 km walk. * If you are using a car, there are over 10 parking spaces next to the temple grounds. === Footnotes === {{Footnote help}} === Notes === {{Notelist2}} === Source === {{Reflist|2}} === References === * Edited by {{jalink|Kenichi Tanigawa|Q2392596}} "Japanese Gods, Shrines and Sacred Places 7 San'in" (newly reprinted) 2000 {{jalink|Hakusuisha|Q4346470}} ISBN 978-4-560-02507-9 === Related items === {{Commonscat|Susa-jinja (Izumo)}} * {{jalink|Suga Shrine|Q11664579}} ({{jalink|Shimane Prefecture|Q132751}}{{jalink|Unnan City|Q877935}}Daito Town) * {{jalink|Hii Shrine|Q11500716}} (Kitsugu-cho, Unnan City, Shimane Prefecture) * {{jalink|Susa Shrine|Q11664499}} (distribution of shrines with the same name) * {{jalink|Sada Town|Q7397459}} * {{jalink|Yukarikan}} === External link === * [http://www.susa-jinja.jp/index.html Susa Shrine] - Official website * [https://twitter.com/susajinjya?ref_src=twsrc%5Egoogle%7Ctwcamp%5Eserp%7Ctwgr%5Eauthor Susa Shrine Official Twitter] * [https://www.izumo-kankou.gr.jp/684 Susa Shrine] - Izumo Tourist Guide / Izumo Tourism Association {{Shinto horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Susashashiya}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Susa Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Susa Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumo Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:島根県の神社]] [[Category:島根県の重要文化財]] [[Category:島根県指定有形文化財]] [[Category:都道府県指定無形民俗文化財]] [[Category:出雲国の式内社]] [[Category:国幣小社]] [[Category:別表神社]] [[Category:出雲市の建築物]] [[Category:出雲市の歴史]] [[Category:大社造]] [[Category:Need translation]] [[Category:kkkkkkkkkkkkkk]] [[Category:nnnnnnnnn]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qq]]

'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '島根県出雲市佐田町宮内730', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Susa-jinja (Izumo)}} [[EN:Susa Shrine]] [[JA:須佐神社 (出雲市)]] [[SV:Susa-jinja]] {{wikidata link|Q11664502}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine | Name=Susa Shrine | Image=[[File:Main Sanctuary of Susa-jinja 001.jpg|Susa Shrine Main Hall|270px]]
Main Hall | Location={{jalink|Shimane Prefecture|Q132751}}{{jalink|Izumo City|Q825166}}730 Susa, Sada Town | Location={{coord|35|14|4.9|N|132|44|12.7|E|region:JP-32_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deity={{jalink|Susanoo|Q272993|Susanoo no Mikoto}} | Shrine rank = {{jalink|Shikiuchi-sha}} (small)
Old {{jalink|Modern shrine rank system #government shrine|Q712534|Kokuhei-kosha}}
{{jalink|Beppyo Shrine|Q10898274}} | Main shrine={{jalink|Taisha-zukuri|Q387874}} | Annual festival={{jalink|April 18th|Q2521}} | Shinto ritual = Nenbutsu dance | Temples, etc.={{jalink|Izumo Kuni Shinto and Buddhist Sacred Sites|Q11395860}}No. 18 | Alias = Susa Omiya|Map={{Location map|Japan Shimane|label=Susa Shrine|lat_deg=35|lat_min=14|lat_sec=4.9|lon_deg=132|lon_min=44|lon_sec=12.7|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point| marker=religious-shinto|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=250|coord={{coord|35|14|4.9|N|132|44|12.7|E|}}}} |Foundation = Age of the Gods}} '''Susa Shrine'' (Susa Jinja) is a {{jalink|Shrine|Q845945}} located in {{jalink|Shimane Prefecture|Q132751}}{{jalink|Izumo City|Q825166}}. An old shrine that enshrines the soul of {{jalink|Susanoo|Q272993|Susanoo no Mikoto}}. {{jalink|Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|Shikinai-kosha}}, and the old {{jalink|shakaku|Q10444029}} is {{jalink|modern company rank system|Q712534|Kokuhei-kosha}}. {{jalink|Izumo Kuni Sacred Sites|Q11395860}}No. 18. === History === In ``{{jalink|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|Q11395858}}'', there is a description that Susanoo no Mikoto came to this area after cultivating various areas, did the final cultivation, and said, ``This is a good country, so I will name the land, not Iwaki,'' and named it ``Susa'' to soothe his soul. Since ancient times, it has been considered the main shrine of Susanoo no Mikoto. The Susa clan of {{jalink|shake|Q11588367}} is said to have descended from {{jalink|Kayanarumi|Q11294873|Kayana-ryu Mime}}, the son of {{jalink|Ōkuninushi}}. The former site of the shrine is said to have been on Mt. Miyao to the north of the shrine. The current shrine site is located almost in the center of the basin, and it is thought that it was already in this area during the Middle Ages. It is described as ``Sosa Shrine'' in ``Izumo no Kuni Fudoki.'' In the ``{{jalink|Engishiki Shinmecho|Q11064932}}'', it is listed as ``Susa Shrine'' and is listed as a small shrine. In the medieval period, it was called ``Jusansho Daimyojin'' and ``Omiya Daimyojin,'' and in the early modern period, it was called ``Susa Omiya.'' In {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}}4 ({{jalink|1871|Q7746}}), the name was changed to "Susa Shrine" as described in the Engishiki, and in Meiji 5 ({{jalink|1872| Q7753}}), it was promoted to a local shrine, the following year in the 6th year of Meiji ({{jalink|1873|Q7757}}), it was promoted to a prefectural shrine, and in the 33rd year of Meiji ({{jalink|1900|Q2034}}) to a national shrine. === Enshrined God === ; Main enshrined deity :* {{jalink|Susanoo|Q272993|Susanoo Mikoto}} ; Deities enshrinedIt is said that the three deities were enshrined at a shrine on the opposite bank of the Susa River (currently within the grounds of Yukari-kan) until the {{jalink|Tenbun (era name)|Q1192853|Tenbun}}. :* {{jalink|Kushinadahime|Q505469|Inadahime no Mikoto}} - Susanoo no Mikoto's wife :* {{jalink|Ashinazuchi Tenazuchi|Q643763|Ashimatsuchi no Mikoto}} - Inadahime no Mikoto's parent :* {{jalink|Ashinazuchi Tenazuchi|Q643763|Temazuchi no Mikoto}} - Inadahime no Mikoto's parent, the ancestor of the Susa family === Facilities === ; Main shrine, Heiden, Worship hall :: The main shrine is a cultural property designated by Shimane Prefecture and was built in 1861. |publisher=Shimane Prefecture Education Agency Cultural Properties Division}}. It is a {{jalink|taisha-zukuri|Q387874}} building with a width of 2 rooms (approximately 4 m) and a height of approximately 12 m, and there is a floor (kizahashi) that serves as the entrance to the 1 room on the right. {{Cite web|Japanese book|url=https://www.susa-jinja.jp/information/8 |title=Gohonden (designated as an important prefectural cultural property) |access-date=2022-07-13 |publisher=Susa Shrine Official}}. The one before that is described as 4 rooms in the Omiyako map written in {{jalink|Gen'o|Q1193925}} 2nd year ({{jalink|1320|Q5768}}). ; Kaguraden ; Shrine office ; salt well:: It is said that Susanoo-no-Mikoto himself drew salt and purified this land. It is said to follow the Sea of ​​Japan, and when the {{jalink|Sea of ​​Japan|Q27092}} is at high tide, tide flowers blow on the ground near the {{jalink|well|Q43483}}. Analysis revealed that it was a "{{jalink|sodium sulfate|Q211737|mirabilite}} containing {{jalink|chloride spring|Q11428612|salt spring}}" and was weakly {{jalink|base|Q11193|alkaline}}{{Cite web|Japanese book|url=https://www.susa-jinja.jp/seven_wonders/1st |title=Shionoi (Shionoi) |access-date=2022-07-13 |publisher=Susa Shrine Official}}. ; Osugi :: Located behind the main shrine, the tree is estimated to be about 1300 years old, with a trunk circumference of 7 meters and a height of 30 meters. Since {{jalink|Hiroyuki Ebara|Q11550701}}'s ``Shinto Travelogue'' was published, many people who were unscrupulous were peeling off the bark of this cedar tree and taking it home with them, so a fence has now been placed around the trunk. File:Torii at the Entrance of Susa-jinja 001.jpg|alt=|Torii at the Entrance of Susa-jinjaFile:Worship Hall of Susa-jinja 001.jpg|alt=|Worship Hall of Susa-jinja File:Heiden Hall (A Hall for Making Offerings to the God) of Susa-jinja 001.jpg|alt=|Heiden Hall File:Kagura Hall (Sacred Dance Hall) in Susa-jinja 001.jpg|alt=|Kagura Hall File:Sacred Saltwater Well in Susa-jinja 001.jpg|alt=|Salt Well File:Sacred Tree (Big Japanese Cedar) in Susa-jinja 001.jpg|alt=|Big Japanese Cedar === Precinct Company === [[File:Main Sanctuary of Auxiliary Amaterasu-jinja in Susa-jinja 001.jpg|thumb|Amaterasusha Main Hall]] ; Amaterasusha :: Deity enshrined: {{jalink|Amaterasu|Q455602}}. It is located across the road in front of the shrine. {{Cite web|Japanese book|url=https://www.susa-jinja.jp/information/25 |title=Susasha |access-date=2022-07-13 |publisher=Susa Shrine Official}}, which is also called "Kami-gozen-san" or "Kami-sha". In {{jalink|Middle Ages|Q12554}} it was called Ise Palace. ; Higashi/Nishisuesha :: Enshrined deity: {{jalink|Amenooshihomimi|Q462555|Amenohomi no Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Amenohohi|Q10940685|Amenohimi no Mikoto}} , {{jalink|Amatsu Hikone Mikoto|Q10940228|Amatsu Hikone Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Ikutsu Hikone Mikoto|Q11147583|Katsu Hikone Mikoto}} , {{jalink|Kumano Kusubi|Q11298796|Kumano Kushihime no Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|Ichikishima Hime no Mikoto} }, {{jalink|Takiribime|Q10932870|Tagitsuhime no Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Tagitsuhime|Q10932592|Tashinhime no Mikoto}}. ; Mihosha:: Deities enshrined: {{jalink|Mihotsuhime|Q6378698|Mihotsu Hime-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Kotoshiro-shu|Q8009611|Kotoshiro-shu-no-Mikoto}}. Also called "Shimo no Gozen-san" or "Shimosha." ; Inarisha :: Enshrined deity: {{jalink|Ukanomitama|Q3080728|Inakura Tama no Mikoto}}. ; Zuishinmon :: Deities enshrined: {{jalink|Amenshimonbetsukami|Q10940542|Toyowamatokami and Kushiwamatokami}}. File:Worship Hall of Auxiliary Amaterasu-jinja in Susa-jinja 001.jpg|alt=|Amaterasusha Worship Hall File:Auxiliary East-jinja in Susa-jinja 001.jpg|alt=|Tosuesha File:Auxiliary West-jinja in Susa-jinja 001.jpg|alt=|Nishisuesha File:Auxiliary Miho-jinja in Susa-jinja 001.jpg|alt=|Mihosha File:Auxiliary Inari-jinja in Susa-jinja 001.jpg|alt=|Inarisha File:Main Gate in Susa-jinja 001.jpg|alt=|Zuishinmon === Kyougaisha === ; Itsukushima Shrine :: Deities enshrined: Ichikishima Hime no Mikoto, Tagoko Hime no Mikoto, Tanatsu Hime no Mikoto ({{jalink|Three goddesses of Munakata|Q10948069}}). Also called ``Seidosha'' and ``Haridosha''. ; Suga Shrine :: Enshrined deity: Suho-no-son. is also called "Saikagura-san". === Festival === * January 1st {{jalink|Saidan Festival|Q11546824}} * February 3rd {{jalink|Setsubun|Q746798|Setsubun Festival}} ** {{jalink|Kagura|Q1348260}} dedication, {{jalink|Great Purification|Q11261910|Chino Ring}}, and bean-throwing will be held. *February 17th Prayer Festival * April 18th {{jalink|Regular Festival|Q11385469}} ** Asashi Matsuri - After the regular festival ritual, the festival is carried out from the main shrine to Amaterasu Shrine across the street. |access-date=2022-07-13 |publisher=Susa Shrine Official}}. * April 19 Koden Festival ** Ryoou-mai - Performed following the Koden Festival ritual. {{Cite web|Japanese book|url=https://www.susa-jinja.jp/event/27/29 |title=Ryoou no Mai |access-date=2022-07-13 |publisher=Susa Shrine Official}}. It is thought that it was originally danced by a divine being of the Onmyoji type, but was transformed into one.** Hundred-handed Shinji - Archery ritual held in the afternoon to pray for the exorcism of demons and a good harvest. * June 30th {{jalink|Great Purification|Q11261910}} * August 15 Kirimei Shinto ritual ** {{jalink|Nenbutsu Dance|Q11491438}} - Held in the afternoon. Two ritual flowers are erected in the shrine grounds, and beneath them dancers wearing kimono (kimono) form a circle, chanting ``Naamamido'' and dancing monotonously to the beat of the flute. It is a dance with strong {{jalink|Shinbutsu syncretism|Q2279088}} color, which is thought to have been influenced by Nembutsu saints in the {{jalink|Dengaku|Q3022455}} style of dance in the medieval period. * October 17th Autumn Festival * November 23rd Niinamatsuri * December 31st Purification * 15th of every month {{jalink|Monthly Festival|Q11516161}} === Cultural assets === === National Important Cultural Property === * Hyogo Kunishitei (unmarked contents) - {{jalink|1912|Q2077}} ({{jalink|Taisho|Q329174}} first year) Designated on September 3rd{{Cite web|Japanese book|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/6331 |title=Hyogo Kunishitei (unwritten contents) / National Treasures and Important Cultural Properties (Artworks) |access-date=2022-07-13 |publisher=Nationally Designated Cultural Properties Database / Agency for Cultural Affairs}}. {{jalink|Tenbun (era name)|Q1192853|Tenbun}} dedicated by {{jalink|Haruhisa Amago|Q3273106}} in 23 ({{jalink|1554|Q6570}}). === Designated by Shimane Prefecture === ; Tangible cultural property * (Building) Main Hall of Susa Shrine - Designated on May 31, {{jalink|1966|Q2649}} ({{jalink|Showa|Q327567}}41). * (Sculpture) Bugaku mask "Nosori" - {{jalink|1972|Q2476}} (Designated on July 28, 1972) |access-date=2022-07-13 |publisher=Shimane Prefecture Education Agency Cultural Properties Division}}. * (Craft) Kurowei Armor Remains - {{jalink|2005|Q2019}} ({{jalink|Heisei|Q234510}}17) Designated April 15th {{Cite web|Japanese book|url=https://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shimane/kougeihin.html |title=List of prefecture-designated cultural properties (crafts) / Cultural properties |access-date=2022-07-13 |publisher=Shimane Prefecture Education Agency Cultural Properties Division}}. ; Intangible folk cultural assets* Nenbutsu dance at Susa Shrine - {{jalink|1961|Q3696}} (Designated on June 13, 1966) |access-date=2022-07-13 |publisher=Shimane Prefecture Education Agency Cultural Properties Division}}. === Transportation === * From {{jalink|Izumo City Station|Q860288}}, take the {{jalink|Ichibata Bus|Q11352955}} bound for Izumo Susa and get off at Susa bus stop. 3 km walk. * If you are using a car, there are over 10 parking spaces next to the temple grounds. === Footnotes === {{Footnote help}} === Notes === {{Notelist2}} === Source === {{Reflist|2}} === References === * Edited by {{jalink|Kenichi Tanigawa|Q2392596}} "Japanese Gods, Shrines and Sacred Places 7 San'in" (newly reprinted) 2000 {{jalink|Hakusuisha|Q4346470}} ISBN 978-4-560-02507-9 === Related items === {{Commonscat|Susa-jinja (Izumo)}} * {{jalink|Suga Shrine|Q11664579}} ({{jalink|Shimane Prefecture|Q132751}}{{jalink|Unnan City|Q877935}}Daito Town) * {{jalink|Hii Shrine|Q11500716}} (Kitsugu-cho, Unnan City, Shimane Prefecture) * {{jalink|Susa Shrine|Q11664499}} (distribution of shrines with the same name) * {{jalink|Sada Town|Q7397459}} * {{jalink|Yukarikan}} === External link === * [http://www.susa-jinja.jp/index.html Susa Shrine] - Official website * [https://twitter.com/susajinjya?ref_src=twsrc%5Egoogle%7Ctwcamp%5Eserp%7Ctwgr%5Eauthor Susa Shrine Official Twitter] * [https://www.izumo-kankou.gr.jp/684 Susa Shrine] - Izumo Tourist Guide / Izumo Tourism Association {{Shinto horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Susashashiya}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Susa Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Susa Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumo Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:島根県の神社]] [[Category:島根県の重要文化財]] [[Category:島根県指定有形文化財]] [[Category:都道府県指定無形民俗文化財]] [[Category:出雲国の式内社]] [[Category:国幣小社]] [[Category:別表神社]] [[Category:出雲市の建築物]] [[Category:出雲市の歴史]] [[Category:大社造]] [[Category:Need translation]] [[Category:kkkkkkkkkkkkkk]] [[Category:nnnnnnnnn]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qq]]

{{jalink|Beppyo Shrine|Q10898274}} | Main shrine={{jalink|Taisha-zukuri|Q387874}} | Annual festival={{jalink|April 18th|Q2521}} | Shinto ritual = Nenbutsu dance | Temples, etc.={{jalink|Izumo Kuni Shinto and Buddhist Sacred Sites|Q11395860}}No. 18 | Alias = Susa Omiya|Map={{Location map|Japan Shimane|label=Susa Shrine|lat_deg=35|lat_min=14|lat_sec=4.9|lon_deg=132|lon_min=44|lon_sec=12.7|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point| marker=religious-shinto|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=250|coord={{coord|35|14|4.9|N|132|44|12.7|E|}}}} |Foundation = Age of the Gods}} '''Susa Shrine'' (Susa Jinja) is a {{jalink|Shrine|Q845945}} located in {{jalink|Shimane Prefecture|Q132751}}{{jalink|Izumo City|Q825166}}. An old shrine that enshrines the soul of {{jalink|Susanoo|Q272993|Susanoo no Mikoto}}. {{jalink|Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|Shikinai-kosha}}, and the old {{jalink|shakaku|Q10444029}} is {{jalink|modern company rank system|Q712534|Kokuhei-kosha}}. {{jalink|Izumo Kuni Sacred Sites|Q11395860}}No. 18. === History === In ``{{jalink|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|Q11395858}}'', there is a description that Susanoo no Mikoto came to this area after cultivating various areas, did the final cultivation, and said, ``This is a good country, so I will name the land, not Iwaki,'' and named it ``Susa'' to soothe his soul. Since ancient times, it has been considered the main shrine of Susanoo no Mikoto. The Susa clan of {{jalink|shake|Q11588367}} is said to have descended from {{jalink|Kayanarumi|Q11294873|Kayana-ryu Mime}}, the son of {{jalink|Ōkuninushi}}. The former site of the shrine is said to have been on Mt. Miyao to the north of the shrine. The current shrine site is located almost in the center of the basin, and it is thought that it was already in this area during the Middle Ages. It is described as ``Sosa Shrine'' in ``Izumo no Kuni Fudoki.'' In the ``{{jalink|Engishiki Shinmecho|Q11064932}}'', it is listed as ``Susa Shrine'' and is listed as a small shrine. In the medieval period, it was called ``Jusansho Daimyojin'' and ``Omiya Daimyojin,'' and in the early modern period, it was called ``Susa Omiya.'' In {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}}4 ({{jalink|1871|Q7746}}), the name was changed to "Susa Shrine" as described in the Engishiki, and in Meiji 5 ({{jalink|1872| Q7753}}), it was promoted to a local shrine, the following year in the 6th year of Meiji ({{jalink|1873|Q7757}}), it was promoted to a prefectural shrine, and in the 33rd year of Meiji ({{jalink|1900|Q2034}}) to a national shrine. === Enshrined God === ; Main enshrined deity :* {{jalink|Susanoo|Q272993|Susanoo Mikoto}} ; Deities enshrinedIt is said that the three deities were enshrined at a shrine on the opposite bank of the Susa River (currently within the grounds of Yukari-kan) until the {{jalink|Tenbun (era name)|Q1192853|Tenbun}}. :* {{jalink|Kushinadahime|Q505469|Inadahime no Mikoto}} - Susanoo no Mikoto's wife :* {{jalink|Ashinazuchi Tenazuchi|Q643763|Ashimatsuchi no Mikoto}} - Inadahime no Mikoto's parent :* {{jalink|Ashinazuchi Tenazuchi|Q643763|Temazuchi no Mikoto}} - Inadahime no Mikoto's parent, the ancestor of the Susa family === Facilities === ; Main shrine, Heiden, Worship hall :: The main shrine is a cultural property designated by Shimane Prefecture and was built in 1861. |publisher=Shimane Prefecture Education Agency Cultural Properties Division}}. It is a {{jalink|taisha-zukuri|Q387874}} building with a width of 2 rooms (approximately 4 m) and a height of approximately 12 m, and there is a floor (kizahashi) that serves as the entrance to the 1 room on the right. {{Cite web|Japanese book|url=https://www.susa-jinja.jp/information/8 |title=Gohonden (designated as an important prefectural cultural property) |access-date=2022-07-13 |publisher=Susa Shrine Official}}. The one before that is described as 4 rooms in the Omiyako map written in {{jalink|Gen'o|Q1193925}} 2nd year ({{jalink|1320|Q5768}}). ; Kaguraden ; Shrine office ; salt well:: It is said that Susanoo-no-Mikoto himself drew salt and purified this land. It is said to follow the Sea of ​​Japan, and when the {{jalink|Sea of ​​Japan|Q27092}} is at high tide, tide flowers blow on the ground near the {{jalink|well|Q43483}}. Analysis revealed that it was a "{{jalink|sodium sulfate|Q211737|mirabilite}} containing {{jalink|chloride spring|Q11428612|salt spring}}" and was weakly {{jalink|base|Q11193|alkaline}}{{Cite web|Japanese book|url=https://www.susa-jinja.jp/seven_wonders/1st |title=Shionoi (Shionoi) |access-date=2022-07-13 |publisher=Susa Shrine Official}}. ; Osugi :: Located behind the main shrine, the tree is estimated to be about 1300 years old, with a trunk circumference of 7 meters and a height of 30 meters. Since {{jalink|Hiroyuki Ebara|Q11550701}}'s ``Shinto Travelogue'' was published, many people who were unscrupulous were peeling off the bark of this cedar tree and taking it home with them, so a fence has now been placed around the trunk. File:Torii at the Entrance of Susa-jinja 001.jpg|alt=|Torii at the Entrance of Susa-jinjaFile:Worship Hall of Susa-jinja 001.jpg|alt=|Worship Hall of Susa-jinja File:Heiden Hall (A Hall for Making Offerings to the God) of Susa-jinja 001.jpg|alt=|Heiden Hall File:Kagura Hall (Sacred Dance Hall) in Susa-jinja 001.jpg|alt=|Kagura Hall File:Sacred Saltwater Well in Susa-jinja 001.jpg|alt=|Salt Well File:Sacred Tree (Big Japanese Cedar) in Susa-jinja 001.jpg|alt=|Big Japanese Cedar === Precinct Company === [[File:Main Sanctuary of Auxiliary Amaterasu-jinja in Susa-jinja 001.jpg|thumb|Amaterasusha Main Hall]] ; Amaterasusha :: Deity enshrined: {{jalink|Amaterasu|Q455602}}. It is located across the road in front of the shrine. {{Cite web|Japanese book|url=https://www.susa-jinja.jp/information/25 |title=Susasha |access-date=2022-07-13 |publisher=Susa Shrine Official}}, which is also called "Kami-gozen-san" or "Kami-sha". In {{jalink|Middle Ages|Q12554}} it was called Ise Palace. ; Higashi/Nishisuesha :: Enshrined deity: {{jalink|Amenooshihomimi|Q462555|Amenohomi no Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Amenohohi|Q10940685|Amenohimi no Mikoto}} , {{jalink|Amatsu Hikone Mikoto|Q10940228|Amatsu Hikone Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Ikutsu Hikone Mikoto|Q11147583|Katsu Hikone Mikoto}} , {{jalink|Kumano Kusubi|Q11298796|Kumano Kushihime no Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|Ichikishima Hime no Mikoto} }, {{jalink|Takiribime|Q10932870|Tagitsuhime no Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Tagitsuhime|Q10932592|Tashinhime no Mikoto}}. ; Mihosha:: Deities enshrined: {{jalink|Mihotsuhime|Q6378698|Mihotsu Hime-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Kotoshiro-shu|Q8009611|Kotoshiro-shu-no-Mikoto}}. Also called "Shimo no Gozen-san" or "Shimosha." ; Inarisha :: Enshrined deity: {{jalink|Ukanomitama|Q3080728|Inakura Tama no Mikoto}}. ; Zuishinmon :: Deities enshrined: {{jalink|Amenshimonbetsukami|Q10940542|Toyowamatokami and Kushiwamatokami}}. File:Worship Hall of Auxiliary Amaterasu-jinja in Susa-jinja 001.jpg|alt=|Amaterasusha Worship Hall File:Auxiliary East-jinja in Susa-jinja 001.jpg|alt=|Tosuesha File:Auxiliary West-jinja in Susa-jinja 001.jpg|alt=|Nishisuesha File:Auxiliary Miho-jinja in Susa-jinja 001.jpg|alt=|Mihosha File:Auxiliary Inari-jinja in Susa-jinja 001.jpg|alt=|Inarisha File:Main Gate in Susa-jinja 001.jpg|alt=|Zuishinmon === Kyougaisha === ; Itsukushima Shrine :: Deities enshrined: Ichikishima Hime no Mikoto, Tagoko Hime no Mikoto, Tanatsu Hime no Mikoto ({{jalink|Three goddesses of Munakata|Q10948069}}). Also called ``Seidosha'' and ``Haridosha''. ; Suga Shrine :: Enshrined deity: Suho-no-son. is also called "Saikagura-san". === Festival === * January 1st {{jalink|Saidan Festival|Q11546824}} * February 3rd {{jalink|Setsubun|Q746798|Setsubun Festival}} ** {{jalink|Kagura|Q1348260}} dedication, {{jalink|Great Purification|Q11261910|Chino Ring}}, and bean-throwing will be held. *February 17th Prayer Festival * April 18th {{jalink|Regular Festival|Q11385469}} ** Asashi Matsuri - After the regular festival ritual, the festival is carried out from the main shrine to Amaterasu Shrine across the street. |access-date=2022-07-13 |publisher=Susa Shrine Official}}. * April 19 Koden Festival ** Ryoou-mai - Performed following the Koden Festival ritual. {{Cite web|Japanese book|url=https://www.susa-jinja.jp/event/27/29 |title=Ryoou no Mai |access-date=2022-07-13 |publisher=Susa Shrine Official}}. It is thought that it was originally danced by a divine being of the Onmyoji type, but was transformed into one.** Hundred-handed Shinji - Archery ritual held in the afternoon to pray for the exorcism of demons and a good harvest. * June 30th {{jalink|Great Purification|Q11261910}} * August 15 Kirimei Shinto ritual ** {{jalink|Nenbutsu Dance|Q11491438}} - Held in the afternoon. Two ritual flowers are erected in the shrine grounds, and beneath them dancers wearing kimono (kimono) form a circle, chanting ``Naamamido'' and dancing monotonously to the beat of the flute. It is a dance with strong {{jalink|Shinbutsu syncretism|Q2279088}} color, which is thought to have been influenced by Nembutsu saints in the {{jalink|Dengaku|Q3022455}} style of dance in the medieval period. * October 17th Autumn Festival * November 23rd Niinamatsuri * December 31st Purification * 15th of every month {{jalink|Monthly Festival|Q11516161}} === Cultural assets === === National Important Cultural Property === * Hyogo Kunishitei (unmarked contents) - {{jalink|1912|Q2077}} ({{jalink|Taisho|Q329174}} first year) Designated on September 3rd{{Cite web|Japanese book|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/6331 |title=Hyogo Kunishitei (unwritten contents) / National Treasures and Important Cultural Properties (Artworks) |access-date=2022-07-13 |publisher=Nationally Designated Cultural Properties Database / Agency for Cultural Affairs}}. {{jalink|Tenbun (era name)|Q1192853|Tenbun}} dedicated by {{jalink|Haruhisa Amago|Q3273106}} in 23 ({{jalink|1554|Q6570}}). === Designated by Shimane Prefecture === ; Tangible cultural property * (Building) Main Hall of Susa Shrine - Designated on May 31, {{jalink|1966|Q2649}} ({{jalink|Showa|Q327567}}41). * (Sculpture) Bugaku mask "Nosori" - {{jalink|1972|Q2476}} (Designated on July 28, 1972) |access-date=2022-07-13 |publisher=Shimane Prefecture Education Agency Cultural Properties Division}}. * (Craft) Kurowei Armor Remains - {{jalink|2005|Q2019}} ({{jalink|Heisei|Q234510}}17) Designated April 15th {{Cite web|Japanese book|url=https://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shimane/kougeihin.html |title=List of prefecture-designated cultural properties (crafts) / Cultural properties |access-date=2022-07-13 |publisher=Shimane Prefecture Education Agency Cultural Properties Division}}. ; Intangible folk cultural assets* Nenbutsu dance at Susa Shrine - {{jalink|1961|Q3696}} (Designated on June 13, 1966) |access-date=2022-07-13 |publisher=Shimane Prefecture Education Agency Cultural Properties Division}}. === Transportation === * From {{jalink|Izumo City Station|Q860288}}, take the {{jalink|Ichibata Bus|Q11352955}} bound for Izumo Susa and get off at Susa bus stop. 3 km walk. * If you are using a car, there are over 10 parking spaces next to the temple grounds. === Footnotes === {{Footnote help}} === Notes === {{Notelist2}} === Source === {{Reflist|2}} === References === * Edited by {{jalink|Kenichi Tanigawa|Q2392596}} "Japanese Gods, Shrines and Sacred Places 7 San'in" (newly reprinted) 2000 {{jalink|Hakusuisha|Q4346470}} ISBN 978-4-560-02507-9 === Related items === {{Commonscat|Susa-jinja (Izumo)}} * {{jalink|Suga Shrine|Q11664579}} ({{jalink|Shimane Prefecture|Q132751}}{{jalink|Unnan City|Q877935}}Daito Town) * {{jalink|Hii Shrine|Q11500716}} (Kitsugu-cho, Unnan City, Shimane Prefecture) * {{jalink|Susa Shrine|Q11664499}} (distribution of shrines with the same name) * {{jalink|Sada Town|Q7397459}} * {{jalink|Yukarikan}} === External link === * [http://www.susa-jinja.jp/index.html Susa Shrine] - An old shrine that enshrines the soul of {{jalink|Susanoo|Q272993|Susanoo no Mikoto}}. {{jalink|Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|Shikinai-kosha}}, and the old {{jalink|shakaku|Q10444029}} is {{jalink|modern company rank system|Q712534|Kokuhei-kosha}}. {{jalink|Izumo Kuni Sacred Sites|Q11395860}}No. 18. === History === In ``{{jalink|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|Q11395858}}'', there is a description that Susanoo no Mikoto came to this area after cultivating various areas, did the final cultivation, and said, ``This is a good country, so I will name the land, not Iwaki,'' and named it ``Susa'' to soothe his soul. Since ancient times, it has been considered the main shrine of Susanoo no Mikoto. The Susa clan of {{jalink|shake|Q11588367}} is said to have descended from {{jalink|Kayanarumi|Q11294873|Kayana-ryu Mime}}, the son of {{jalink|Ōkuninushi}}. The former site of the shrine is said to have been on Mt. Miyao to the north of the shrine. The current shrine site is located almost in the center of the basin, and it is thought that it was already in this area during the Middle Ages. It is described as ``Sosa Shrine'' in ``Izumo no Kuni Fudoki.'' In the ``{{jalink|Engishiki Shinmecho|Q11064932}}'', it is listed as ``Susa Shrine'' and is listed as a small shrine. In the medieval period, it was called ``Jusansho Daimyojin'' and ``Omiya Daimyojin,'' and in the early modern period, it was called ``Susa Omiya.'' In {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}}4 ({{jalink|1871|Q7746}}), the name was changed to "Susa Shrine" as described in the Engishiki, and in Meiji 5 ({{jalink|1872| Q7753}}), it was promoted to a local shrine, the following year in the 6th year of Meiji ({{jalink|1873|Q7757}}), it was promoted to a prefectural shrine, and in the 33rd year of Meiji ({{jalink|1900|Q2034}}) to a national shrine. === Enshrined God === ; Main enshrined deity :* {{jalink|Susanoo|Q272993|Susanoo Mikoto}} ; Deities enshrinedIt is said that the three deities were enshrined at a shrine on the opposite bank of the Susa River (currently within the grounds of Yukari-kan) until the {{jalink|Tenbun (era name)|Q1192853|Tenbun}}. :* {{jalink|Kushinadahime|Q505469|Inadahime no Mikoto}} - Susanoo no Mikoto's wife :* {{jalink|Ashinazuchi Tenazuchi|Q643763|Ashimatsuchi no Mikoto}} - Inadahime no Mikoto's parent :* {{jalink|Ashinazuchi Tenazuchi|Q643763|Temazuchi no Mikoto}} - Inadahime no Mikoto's parent, the ancestor of the Susa family === Facilities === ; Main shrine, Heiden, Worship hall :: The main shrine is a cultural property designated by Shimane Prefecture and was built in 1861. |publisher=Shimane Prefecture Education Agency Cultural Properties Division}}. It is a {{jalink|taisha-zukuri|Q387874}} building with a width of 2 rooms (approximately 4 m) and a height of approximately 12 m, and there is a floor (kizahashi) that serves as the entrance to the 1 room on the right. {{Cite web|Japanese book|url=https://www.susa-jinja.jp/information/8 |title=Gohonden (designated as an important prefectural cultural property) |access-date=2022-07-13 |publisher=Susa Shrine Official}}. The one before that is described as 4 rooms in the Omiyako map written in {{jalink|Gen'o|Q1193925}} 2nd year ({{jalink|1320|Q5768}}). ; Kaguraden ; Shrine office ; salt well:: It is said that Susanoo-no-Mikoto himself drew salt and purified this land. It is said to follow the Sea of ​​Japan, and when the {{jalink|Sea of ​​Japan|Q27092}} is at high tide, tide flowers blow on the ground near the {{jalink|well|Q43483}}. Analysis revealed that it was a "{{jalink|sodium sulfate|Q211737|mirabilite}} containing {{jalink|chloride spring|Q11428612|salt spring}}" and was weakly {{jalink|base|Q11193|alkaline}}{{Cite web|Japanese book|url=https://www.susa-jinja.jp/seven_wonders/1st |title=Shionoi (Shionoi) |access-date=2022-07-13 |publisher=Susa Shrine Official}}. ; Osugi :: Located behind the main shrine, the tree is estimated to be about 1300 years old, with a trunk circumference of 7 meters and a height of 30 meters. Since {{jalink|Hiroyuki Ebara|Q11550701}}'s ``Shinto Travelogue'' was published, many people who were unscrupulous were peeling off the bark of this cedar tree and taking it home with them, so a fence has now been placed around the trunk. File:Torii at the Entrance of Susa-jinja 001.jpg|alt=|Torii at the Entrance of Susa-jinjaFile:Worship Hall of Susa-jinja 001.jpg|alt=|Worship Hall of Susa-jinja File:Heiden Hall (A Hall for Making Offerings to the God) of Susa-jinja 001.jpg|alt=|Heiden Hall File:Kagura Hall (Sacred Dance Hall) in Susa-jinja 001.jpg|alt=|Kagura Hall File:Sacred Saltwater Well in Susa-jinja 001.jpg|alt=|Salt Well File:Sacred Tree (Big Japanese Cedar) in Susa-jinja 001.jpg|alt=|Big Japanese Cedar === Precinct Company === [[File:Main Sanctuary of Auxiliary Amaterasu-jinja in Susa-jinja 001.jpg|thumb|Amaterasusha Main Hall]] ; Amaterasusha :: Deity enshrined: {{jalink|Amaterasu|Q455602}}. It is located across the road in front of the shrine. {{Cite web|Japanese book|url=https://www.susa-jinja.jp/information/25 |title=Susasha |access-date=2022-07-13 |publisher=Susa Shrine Official}}, which is also called "Kami-gozen-san" or "Kami-sha". In {{jalink|Middle Ages|Q12554}} it was called Ise Palace. ; Higashi/Nishisuesha :: Enshrined deity: {{jalink|Amenooshihomimi|Q462555|Amenohomi no Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Amenohohi|Q10940685|Amenohimi no Mikoto}} , {{jalink|Amatsu Hikone Mikoto|Q10940228|Amatsu Hikone Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Ikutsu Hikone Mikoto|Q11147583|Katsu Hikone Mikoto}} , {{jalink|Kumano Kusubi|Q11298796|Kumano Kushihime no Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|Ichikishima Hime no Mikoto} }, {{jalink|Takiribime|Q10932870|Tagitsuhime no Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Tagitsuhime|Q10932592|Tashinhime no Mikoto}}. ; Mihosha:: Deities enshrined: {{jalink|Mihotsuhime|Q6378698|Mihotsu Hime-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Kotoshiro-shu|Q8009611|Kotoshiro-shu-no-Mikoto}}. Also called "Shimo no Gozen-san" or "Shimosha." ; Inarisha :: Enshrined deity: {{jalink|Ukanomitama|Q3080728|Inakura Tama no Mikoto}}. ; Zuishinmon :: Deities enshrined: {{jalink|Amenshimonbetsukami|Q10940542|Toyowamatokami and Kushiwamatokami}}. File:Worship Hall of Auxiliary Amaterasu-jinja in Susa-jinja 001.jpg|alt=|Amaterasusha Worship Hall File:Auxiliary East-jinja in Susa-jinja 001.jpg|alt=|Tosuesha File:Auxiliary West-jinja in Susa-jinja 001.jpg|alt=|Nishisuesha File:Auxiliary Miho-jinja in Susa-jinja 001.jpg|alt=|Mihosha File:Auxiliary Inari-jinja in Susa-jinja 001.jpg|alt=|Inarisha File:Main Gate in Susa-jinja 001.jpg|alt=|Zuishinmon === Kyougaisha === ; Itsukushima Shrine :: Deities enshrined: Ichikishima Hime no Mikoto, Tagoko Hime no Mikoto, Tanatsu Hime no Mikoto ({{jalink|Three goddesses of Munakata|Q10948069}}). Also called ``Seidosha'' and ``Haridosha''. ; Suga Shrine :: Enshrined deity: Suho-no-son. is also called "Saikagura-san". === Festival === * January 1st {{jalink|Saidan Festival|Q11546824}} * February 3rd {{jalink|Setsubun|Q746798|Setsubun Festival}} ** {{jalink|Kagura|Q1348260}} dedication, {{jalink|Great Purification|Q11261910|Chino Ring}}, and bean-throwing will be held. *February 17th Prayer Festival * April 18th {{jalink|Regular Festival|Q11385469}} ** Asashi Matsuri - After the regular festival ritual, the festival is carried out from the main shrine to Amaterasu Shrine across the street. |access-date=2022-07-13 |publisher=Susa Shrine Official}}. * April 19 Koden Festival ** Ryoou-mai - Performed following the Koden Festival ritual. {{Cite web|Japanese book|url=https://www.susa-jinja.jp/event/27/29 |title=Ryoou no Mai |access-date=2022-07-13 |publisher=Susa Shrine Official}}. It is thought that it was originally danced by a divine being of the Onmyoji type, but was transformed into one.** Hundred-handed Shinji - Archery ritual held in the afternoon to pray for the exorcism of demons and a good harvest. * June 30th {{jalink|Great Purification|Q11261910}} * August 15 Kirimei Shinto ritual ** {{jalink|Nenbutsu Dance|Q11491438}} - Held in the afternoon. Two ritual flowers are erected in the shrine grounds, and beneath them dancers wearing kimono (kimono) form a circle, chanting ``Naamamido'' and dancing monotonously to the beat of the flute. It is a dance with strong {{jalink|Shinbutsu syncretism|Q2279088}} color, which is thought to have been influenced by Nembutsu saints in the {{jalink|Dengaku|Q3022455}} style of dance in the medieval period. * October 17th Autumn Festival * November 23rd Niinamatsuri * December 31st Purification * 15th of every month {{jalink|Monthly Festival|Q11516161}} === Cultural assets === === National Important Cultural Property === * Hyogo Kunishitei (unmarked contents) - {{jalink|1912|Q2077}} ({{jalink|Taisho|Q329174}} first year) Designated on September 3rd{{Cite web|Japanese book|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/6331 |title=Hyogo Kunishitei (unwritten contents) / National Treasures and Important Cultural Properties (Artworks) |access-date=2022-07-13 |publisher=Nationally Designated Cultural Properties Database / Agency for Cultural Affairs}}. {{jalink|Tenbun (era name)|Q1192853|Tenbun}} dedicated by {{jalink|Haruhisa Amago|Q3273106}} in 23 ({{jalink|1554|Q6570}}). === Designated by Shimane Prefecture === ; Tangible cultural property * (Building) Main Hall of Susa Shrine - Designated on May 31, {{jalink|1966|Q2649}} ({{jalink|Showa|Q327567}}41). * (Sculpture) Bugaku mask "Nosori" - {{jalink|1972|Q2476}} (Designated on July 28, 1972) |access-date=2022-07-13 |publisher=Shimane Prefecture Education Agency Cultural Properties Division}}. * (Craft) Kurowei Armor Remains - {{jalink|2005|Q2019}} ({{jalink|Heisei|Q234510}}17) Designated April 15th {{Cite web|Japanese book|url=https://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shimane/kougeihin.html |title=List of prefecture-designated cultural properties (crafts) / Cultural properties |access-date=2022-07-13 |publisher=Shimane Prefecture Education Agency Cultural Properties Division}}. ; Intangible folk cultural assets* Nenbutsu dance at Susa Shrine - {{jalink|1961|Q3696}} (Designated on June 13, 1966) |access-date=2022-07-13 |publisher=Shimane Prefecture Education Agency Cultural Properties Division}}. === Transportation === * From {{jalink|Izumo City Station|Q860288}}, take the {{jalink|Ichibata Bus|Q11352955}} bound for Izumo Susa and get off at Susa bus stop. 3 km walk. * If you are using a car, there are over 10 parking spaces next to the temple grounds. === Footnotes === {{Footnote help}} === Notes === {{Notelist2}} === Source === {{Reflist|2}} === References === * Edited by {{jalink|Kenichi Tanigawa|Q2392596}} "Japanese Gods, Shrines and Sacred Places 7 San'in" (newly reprinted) 2000 {{jalink|Hakusuisha|Q4346470}} ISBN 978-4-560-02507-9 === Related items === {{Commonscat|Susa-jinja (Izumo)}} * {{jalink|Suga Shrine|Q11664579}} ({{jalink|Shimane Prefecture|Q132751}}{{jalink|Unnan City|Q877935}}Daito Town) * {{jalink|Hii Shrine|Q11500716}} (Kitsugu-cho, Unnan City, Shimane Prefecture) * {{jalink|Susa Shrine|Q11664499}} (distribution of shrines with the same name) * {{jalink|Sada Town|Q7397459}} * {{jalink|Yukarikan}} === External link === * [http://www.susa-jinja.jp/index.html Susa Shrine] - {{jalink|Beppyo Shrine|Q10898274}} | Main shrine={{jalink|Taisha-zukuri|Q387874}} | Annual festival={{jalink|April 18th|Q2521}} | Shinto ritual = Nenbutsu dance | Temples, etc.={{jalink|Izumo Kuni Shinto and Buddhist Sacred Sites|Q11395860}}No. 18 | Alias = Susa Omiya|Map={{Location map|Japan Shimane|label=Susa Shrine|lat_deg=35|lat_min=14|lat_sec=4.9|lon_deg=132|lon_min=44|lon_sec=12.7|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point| marker=religious-shinto|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=250|coord={{coord|35|14|4.9|N|132|44|12.7|E|}}}} |Foundation = Age of the Gods}} '''Susa Shrine'' (Susa Jinja) is a {{jalink|Shrine|Q845945}} located in {{jalink|Shimane Prefecture|Q132751}}{{jalink|Izumo City|Q825166}}. An old shrine that enshrines the soul of {{jalink|Susanoo|Q272993|Susanoo no Mikoto}}. {{jalink|Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|Shikinai-kosha}}, and the old {{jalink|shakaku|Q10444029}} is {{jalink|modern company rank system|Q712534|Kokuhei-kosha}}. {{jalink|Izumo Kuni Sacred Sites|Q11395860}}No. 18. === History === In ``{{jalink|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|Q11395858}}'', there is a description that Susanoo no Mikoto came to this area after cultivating various areas, did the final cultivation, and said, ``This is a good country, so I will name the land, not Iwaki,'' and named it ``Susa'' to soothe his soul. Since ancient times, it has been considered the main shrine of Susanoo no Mikoto. The Susa clan of {{jalink|shake|Q11588367}} is said to have descended from {{jalink|Kayanarumi|Q11294873|Kayana-ryu Mime}}, the son of {{jalink|Ōkuninushi}}. The former site of the shrine is said to have been on Mt. Miyao to the north of the shrine. The current shrine site is located almost in the center of the basin, and it is thought that it was already in this area during the Middle Ages. It is described as ``Sosa Shrine'' in ``Izumo no Kuni Fudoki.'' In the ``{{jalink|Engishiki Shinmecho|Q11064932}}'', it is listed as ``Susa Shrine'' and is listed as a small shrine. In the medieval period, it was called ``Jusansho Daimyojin'' and ``Omiya Daimyojin,'' and in the early modern period, it was called ``Susa Omiya.'' In {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}}4 ({{jalink|1871|Q7746}}), the name was changed to "Susa Shrine" as described in the Engishiki, and in Meiji 5 ({{jalink|1872| Q7753}}), it was promoted to a local shrine, the following year in the 6th year of Meiji ({{jalink|1873|Q7757}}), it was promoted to a prefectural shrine, and in the 33rd year of Meiji ({{jalink|1900|Q2034}}) to a national shrine. === Enshrined God === ; Main enshrined deity :* {{jalink|Susanoo|Q272993|Susanoo Mikoto}} ; Deities enshrinedIt is said that the three deities were enshrined at a shrine on the opposite bank of the Susa River (currently within the grounds of Yukari-kan) until the {{jalink|Tenbun (era name)|Q1192853|Tenbun}}. :* {{jalink|Kushinadahime|Q505469|Inadahime no Mikoto}} - Susanoo no Mikoto's wife :* {{jalink|Ashinazuchi Tenazuchi|Q643763|Ashimatsuchi no Mikoto}} - Inadahime no Mikoto's parent :* {{jalink|Ashinazuchi Tenazuchi|Q643763|Temazuchi no Mikoto}} - Inadahime no Mikoto's parent, the ancestor of the Susa family === Facilities === ; Main shrine, Heiden, Worship hall :: The main shrine is a cultural property designated by Shimane Prefecture and was built in 1861. |publisher=Shimane Prefecture Education Agency Cultural Properties Division}}. It is a {{jalink|taisha-zukuri|Q387874}} building with a width of 2 rooms (approximately 4 m) and a height of approximately 12 m, and there is a floor (kizahashi) that serves as the entrance to the 1 room on the right. {{Cite web|Japanese book|url=https://www.susa-jinja.jp/information/8 |title=Gohonden (designated as an important prefectural cultural property) |access-date=2022-07-13 |publisher=Susa Shrine Official}}. The one before that is described as 4 rooms in the Omiyako map written in {{jalink|Gen'o|Q1193925}} 2nd year ({{jalink|1320|Q5768}}). ; Kaguraden ; Shrine office ; salt well:: It is said that Susanoo-no-Mikoto himself drew salt and purified this land. It is said to follow the Sea of ​​Japan, and when the {{jalink|Sea of ​​Japan|Q27092}} is at high tide, tide flowers blow on the ground near the {{jalink|well|Q43483}}. Analysis revealed that it was a "{{jalink|sodium sulfate|Q211737|mirabilite}} containing {{jalink|chloride spring|Q11428612|salt spring}}" and was weakly {{jalink|base|Q11193|alkaline}}{{Cite web|Japanese book|url=https://www.susa-jinja.jp/seven_wonders/1st |title=Shionoi (Shionoi) |access-date=2022-07-13 |publisher=Susa Shrine Official}}. ; Osugi :: Located behind the main shrine, the tree is estimated to be about 1300 years old, with a trunk circumference of 7 meters and a height of 30 meters. Since {{jalink|Hiroyuki Ebara|Q11550701}}'s ``Shinto Travelogue'' was published, many people who were unscrupulous were peeling off the bark of this cedar tree and taking it home with them, so a fence has now been placed around the trunk. File:Torii at the Entrance of Susa-jinja 001.jpg|alt=|Torii at the Entrance of Susa-jinjaFile:Worship Hall of Susa-jinja 001.jpg|alt=|Worship Hall of Susa-jinja File:Heiden Hall (A Hall for Making Offerings to the God) of Susa-jinja 001.jpg|alt=|Heiden Hall File:Kagura Hall (Sacred Dance Hall) in Susa-jinja 001.jpg|alt=|Kagura Hall File:Sacred Saltwater Well in Susa-jinja 001.jpg|alt=|Salt Well File:Sacred Tree (Big Japanese Cedar) in Susa-jinja 001.jpg|alt=|Big Japanese Cedar === Precinct Company === [[File:Main Sanctuary of Auxiliary Amaterasu-jinja in Susa-jinja 001.jpg|thumb|Amaterasusha Main Hall]] ; Amaterasusha :: Deity enshrined: {{jalink|Amaterasu|Q455602}}. It is located across the road in front of the shrine. {{Cite web|Japanese book|url=https://www.susa-jinja.jp/information/25 |title=Susasha |access-date=2022-07-13 |publisher=Susa Shrine Official}}, which is also called "Kami-gozen-san" or "Kami-sha". In {{jalink|Middle Ages|Q12554}} it was called Ise Palace. ; Higashi/Nishisuesha :: Enshrined deity: {{jalink|Amenooshihomimi|Q462555|Amenohomi no Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Amenohohi|Q10940685|Amenohimi no Mikoto}} , {{jalink|Amatsu Hikone Mikoto|Q10940228|Amatsu Hikone Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Ikutsu Hikone Mikoto|Q11147583|Katsu Hikone Mikoto}} , {{jalink|Kumano Kusubi|Q11298796|Kumano Kushihime no Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|Ichikishima Hime no Mikoto} }, {{jalink|Takiribime|Q10932870|Tagitsuhime no Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Tagitsuhime|Q10932592|Tashinhime no Mikoto}}. ; Mihosha:: Deities enshrined: {{jalink|Mihotsuhime|Q6378698|Mihotsu Hime-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Kotoshiro-shu|Q8009611|Kotoshiro-shu-no-Mikoto}}. Also called "Shimo no Gozen-san" or "Shimosha." ; Inarisha :: Enshrined deity: {{jalink|Ukanomitama|Q3080728|Inakura Tama no Mikoto}}. ; Zuishinmon :: Deities enshrined: {{jalink|Amenshimonbetsukami|Q10940542|Toyowamatokami and Kushiwamatokami}}. File:Worship Hall of Auxiliary Amaterasu-jinja in Susa-jinja 001.jpg|alt=|Amaterasusha Worship Hall File:Auxiliary East-jinja in Susa-jinja 001.jpg|alt=|Tosuesha File:Auxiliary West-jinja in Susa-jinja 001.jpg|alt=|Nishisuesha File:Auxiliary Miho-jinja in Susa-jinja 001.jpg|alt=|Mihosha File:Auxiliary Inari-jinja in Susa-jinja 001.jpg|alt=|Inarisha File:Main Gate in Susa-jinja 001.jpg|alt=|Zuishinmon === Kyougaisha === ; Itsukushima Shrine :: Deities enshrined: Ichikishima Hime no Mikoto, Tagoko Hime no Mikoto, Tanatsu Hime no Mikoto ({{jalink|Three goddesses of Munakata|Q10948069}}). Also called ``Seidosha'' and ``Haridosha''. ; Suga Shrine :: Enshrined deity: Suho-no-son. is also called "Saikagura-san". === Festival === * January 1st {{jalink|Saidan Festival|Q11546824}} * February 3rd {{jalink|Setsubun|Q746798|Setsubun Festival}} ** {{jalink|Kagura|Q1348260}} dedication, {{jalink|Great Purification|Q11261910|Chino Ring}}, and bean-throwing will be held. *February 17th Prayer Festival * April 18th {{jalink|Regular Festival|Q11385469}} ** Asashi Matsuri - After the regular festival ritual, the festival is carried out from the main shrine to Amaterasu Shrine across the street. |access-date=2022-07-13 |publisher=Susa Shrine Official}}. * April 19 Koden Festival ** Ryoou-mai - Performed following the Koden Festival ritual. {{Cite web|Japanese book|url=https://www.susa-jinja.jp/event/27/29 |title=Ryoou no Mai |access-date=2022-07-13 |publisher=Susa Shrine Official}}. It is thought that it was originally danced by a divine being of the Onmyoji type, but was transformed into one.** Hundred-handed Shinji - Archery ritual held in the afternoon to pray for the exorcism of demons and a good harvest. * June 30th {{jalink|Great Purification|Q11261910}} * August 15 Kirimei Shinto ritual ** {{jalink|Nenbutsu Dance|Q11491438}} - Held in the afternoon. Two ritual flowers are erected in the shrine grounds, and beneath them dancers wearing kimono (kimono) form a circle, chanting ``Naamamido'' and dancing monotonously to the beat of the flute. It is a dance with strong {{jalink|Shinbutsu syncretism|Q2279088}} color, which is thought to have been influenced by Nembutsu saints in the {{jalink|Dengaku|Q3022455}} style of dance in the medieval period. * October 17th Autumn Festival * November 23rd Niinamatsuri * December 31st Purification * 15th of every month {{jalink|Monthly Festival|Q11516161}} === Cultural assets === === National Important Cultural Property === * Hyogo Kunishitei (unmarked contents) - {{jalink|1912|Q2077}} ({{jalink|Taisho|Q329174}} first year) Designated on September 3rd{{Cite web|Japanese book|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/6331 |title=Hyogo Kunishitei (unwritten contents) / National Treasures and Important Cultural Properties (Artworks) |access-date=2022-07-13 |publisher=Nationally Designated Cultural Properties Database / Agency for Cultural Affairs}}. {{jalink|Tenbun (era name)|Q1192853|Tenbun}} dedicated by {{jalink|Haruhisa Amago|Q3273106}} in 23 ({{jalink|1554|Q6570}}). === Designated by Shimane Prefecture === ; Tangible cultural property * (Building) Main Hall of Susa Shrine - Designated on May 31, {{jalink|1966|Q2649}} ({{jalink|Showa|Q327567}}41). * (Sculpture) Bugaku mask "Nosori" - {{jalink|1972|Q2476}} (Designated on July 28, 1972) |access-date=2022-07-13 |publisher=Shimane Prefecture Education Agency Cultural Properties Division}}. * (Craft) Kurowei Armor Remains - {{jalink|2005|Q2019}} ({{jalink|Heisei|Q234510}}17) Designated April 15th {{Cite web|Japanese book|url=https://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shimane/kougeihin.html |title=List of prefecture-designated cultural properties (crafts) / Cultural properties |access-date=2022-07-13 |publisher=Shimane Prefecture Education Agency Cultural Properties Division}}. ; Intangible folk cultural assets* Nenbutsu dance at Susa Shrine - {{jalink|1961|Q3696}} (Designated on June 13, 1966) |access-date=2022-07-13 |publisher=Shimane Prefecture Education Agency Cultural Properties Division}}. === Transportation === * From {{jalink|Izumo City Station|Q860288}}, take the {{jalink|Ichibata Bus|Q11352955}} bound for Izumo Susa and get off at Susa bus stop. 3 km walk. * If you are using a car, there are over 10 parking spaces next to the temple grounds. === Footnotes === {{Footnote help}} === Notes === {{Notelist2}} === Source === {{Reflist|2}} === References === * Edited by {{jalink|Kenichi Tanigawa|Q2392596}} "Japanese Gods, Shrines and Sacred Places 7 San'in" (newly reprinted) 2000 {{jalink|Hakusuisha|Q4346470}} ISBN 978-4-560-02507-9 === Related items === {{Commonscat|Susa-jinja (Izumo)}} * {{jalink|Suga Shrine|Q11664579}} ({{jalink|Shimane Prefecture|Q132751}}{{jalink|Unnan City|Q877935}}Daito Town) * {{jalink|Hii Shrine|Q11500716}} (Kitsugu-cho, Unnan City, Shimane Prefecture) * {{jalink|Susa Shrine|Q11664499}} (distribution of shrines with the same name) * {{jalink|Sada Town|Q7397459}} * {{jalink|Yukarikan}} === External link === * [http://www.susa-jinja.jp/index.html Susa Shrine] - Old {{ill|lt=Modern shrine rank system #government shrine|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=Kokuhei-kosha}}
{{ill|lt=Beppyo Shrine|Q10898274|WD=Q10898274}} | Main shrine={{ill|lt=Taisha-zukuri|Q387874|WD=Q387874}} | Annual festival={{ill|lt=April 18th|Q2521|WD=Q2521}} | Shinto ritual = Nenbutsu dance | Temples, etc.={{ill|lt=Izumo Kuni Shinto and Buddhist Sacred Sites|Q11395860|WD=Q11395860}}No. 18 | Alias = Susa Omiya|Map={{Location map|Japan Shimane|label=Susa Shrine|lat_deg=35|lat_min=14|lat_sec=4.9|lon_deg=132|lon_min=44|lon_sec=12.7|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point| marker=religious-shinto|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=250|coord={{coord|35|14|4.9|N|132|44|12.7|E|}}}} |Foundation = Age of the Gods}} '''Susa Shrine'' (Susa Jinja) is a {{ill|lt=Shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751}}{{ill|lt=Izumo City|Q825166|WD=Q825166}}. An old shrine that enshrines the soul of {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo no Mikoto}}. {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinai-kosha}}, and the old {{ill|lt=shakaku|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029}} is {{ill|lt=modern company rank system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=Kokuhei-kosha}}. {{ill|lt=Izumo Kuni Sacred Sites|Q11395860|WD=Q11395860}}No. 18. === History === In ``{{ill|lt=Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|Q11395858|WD=Q11395858}}'', there is a description that Susanoo no Mikoto came to this area after cultivating various areas, did the final cultivation, and said, ``This is a good country, so I will name the land, not Iwaki,'' and named it ``Susa'' to soothe his soul. Since ancient times, it has been considered the main shrine of Susanoo no Mikoto. The Susa clan of {{ill|lt=shake|Q11588367|WD=Q11588367}} is said to have descended from {{ill|lt=Kayanarumi|Q11294873|WD=Q11294873|lt=Kayana-ryu Mime}}, the son of {{ill||lt=Ōkuninushi|UNKNOWN}}. The former site of the shrine is said to have been on Mt. Miyao to the north of the shrine. The current shrine site is located almost in the center of the basin, and it is thought that it was already in this area during the Middle Ages. It is described as ``Sosa Shrine'' in ``Izumo no Kuni Fudoki.'' In the ``{{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmecho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}'', it is listed as ``Susa Shrine'' and is listed as a small shrine. In the medieval period, it was called ``Jusansho Daimyojin'' and ``Omiya Daimyojin,'' and in the early modern period, it was called ``Susa Omiya.'' In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}}4 ({{ill|lt=1871|Q7746|WD=Q7746}}), the name was changed to "Susa Shrine" as described in the Engishiki, and in Meiji 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753}}), it was promoted to a local shrine, the following year in the 6th year of Meiji ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757}}), it was promoted to a prefectural shrine, and in the 33rd year of Meiji ({{ill|lt=1900|Q2034|WD=Q2034}}) to a national shrine. === Enshrined God === ; Main enshrined deity :* {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo Mikoto}} ; Deities enshrinedIt is said that the three deities were enshrined at a shrine on the opposite bank of the Susa River (currently within the grounds of Yukari-kan) until the {{ill|lt=Tenbun (era name)|Q1192853|WD=Q1192853|lt=Tenbun}}. :* {{ill|lt=Kushinadahime|Q505469|WD=Q505469|lt=Inadahime no Mikoto}} - Susanoo no Mikoto's wife :* {{ill|lt=Ashinazuchi Tenazuchi|Q643763|WD=Q643763|lt=Ashimatsuchi no Mikoto}} - Inadahime no Mikoto's parent :* {{ill|lt=Ashinazuchi Tenazuchi|Q643763|WD=Q643763|lt=Temazuchi no Mikoto}} - Inadahime no Mikoto's parent, the ancestor of the Susa family === Facilities === ; Main shrine, Heiden, Worship hall :: The main shrine is a cultural property designated by Shimane Prefecture and was built in 1861. |publisher=Shimane Prefecture Education Agency Cultural Properties Division}}. It is a {{ill|lt=taisha-zukuri|Q387874|WD=Q387874}} building with a width of 2 rooms (approximately 4 m) and a height of approximately 12 m, and there is a floor (kizahashi) that serves as the entrance to the 1 room on the right. {{Cite web|Japanese book|url=https://www.susa-jinja.jp/information/8 |title=Gohonden (designated as an important prefectural cultural property) |access-date=2022-07-13 |publisher=Susa Shrine Official}}. The one before that is described as 4 rooms in the Omiyako map written in {{ill|lt=Gen'o|Q1193925|WD=Q1193925}} 2nd year ({{ill|lt=1320|Q5768|WD=Q5768}}). ; Kaguraden ; Shrine office ; salt well:: It is said that Susanoo-no-Mikoto himself drew salt and purified this land. It is said to follow the Sea of ​​Japan, and when the {{ill|lt=Sea of ​​Japan|Q27092|WD=Q27092}} is at high tide, tide flowers blow on the ground near the {{ill|lt=well|Q43483|WD=Q43483}}. Analysis revealed that it was a "{{ill|lt=sodium sulfate|Q211737|WD=Q211737|lt=mirabilite}} containing {{ill|lt=chloride spring|Q11428612|WD=Q11428612|lt=salt spring}}" and was weakly {{ill|lt=base|Q11193|WD=Q11193|lt=alkaline}}{{Cite web|Japanese book|url=https://www.susa-jinja.jp/seven_wonders/1st |title=Shionoi (Shionoi) |access-date=2022-07-13 |publisher=Susa Shrine Official}}. ; Osugi :: Located behind the main shrine, the tree is estimated to be about 1300 years old, with a trunk circumference of 7 meters and a height of 30 meters. Since {{ill|lt=Hiroyuki Ebara|Q11550701|WD=Q11550701}}'s ``Shinto Travelogue'' was published, many people who were unscrupulous were peeling off the bark of this cedar tree and taking it home with them, so a fence has now been placed around the trunk. It is located across the road in front of the shrine. {{Cite web|Japanese book|url=https://www.susa-jinja.jp/information/25 |title=Susasha |access-date=2022-07-13 |publisher=Susa Shrine Official}}, which is also called "Kami-gozen-san" or "Kami-sha". In {{ill|lt=Middle Ages|Q12554|WD=Q12554}} it was called Ise Palace. ; Higashi/Nishisuesha :: Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Amenooshihomimi|Q462555|WD=Q462555|lt=Amenohomi no Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Amenohohi|Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|lt=Amenohimi no Mikoto}} , {{ill|lt=Amatsu Hikone Mikoto|Q10940228|WD=Q10940228|lt=Amatsu Hikone Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Ikutsu Hikone Mikoto|Q11147583|WD=Q11147583|lt=Katsu Hikone Mikoto}} , {{ill|lt=Kumano Kusubi|Q11298796|WD=Q11298796|lt=Kumano Kushihime no Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|Ichikishima Hime no Mikoto} }, {{ill|lt=Takiribime|Q10932870|WD=Q10932870|lt=Tagitsuhime no Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Tagitsuhime|Q10932592|WD=Q10932592|lt=Tashinhime no Mikoto}}. ; Mihosha:: Deities enshrined: {{ill|lt=Mihotsuhime|Q6378698|WD=Q6378698|lt=Mihotsu Hime-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Kotoshiro-shu|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshiro-shu-no-Mikoto}}. Also called "Shimo no Gozen-san" or "Shimosha." ; Inarisha :: Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Inakura Tama no Mikoto}}. ; Zuishinmon :: Deities enshrined: {{ill|lt=Amenshimonbetsukami|Q10940542|WD=Q10940542|lt=Toyowamatokami and Kushiwamatokami}}. File:Worship Hall of Auxiliary Amaterasu-jinja in Susa-jinja 001.jpg|alt=|Amaterasusha Worship Hall File:Auxiliary East-jinja in Susa-jinja 001.jpg|alt=|Tosuesha File:Auxiliary West-jinja in Susa-jinja 001.jpg|alt=|Nishisuesha File:Auxiliary Miho-jinja in Susa-jinja 001.jpg|alt=|Mihosha File:Auxiliary Inari-jinja in Susa-jinja 001.jpg|alt=|Inarisha File:Main Gate in Susa-jinja 001.jpg|alt=|Zuishinmon === Kyougaisha === ; Itsukushima Shrine :: Deities enshrined: Ichikishima Hime no Mikoto, Tagoko Hime no Mikoto, Tanatsu Hime no Mikoto ({{ill|lt=Three goddesses of Munakata|Q10948069|WD=Q10948069}}). Also called ``Seidosha'' and ``Haridosha''. ; Suga Shrine :: Enshrined deity: Suho-no-son. is also called "Saikagura-san". === Festival === * January 1st {{ill|lt=Saidan Festival|Q11546824|WD=Q11546824}} * February 3rd {{ill|lt=Setsubun|Q746798|WD=Q746798|lt=Setsubun Festival}} ** {{ill|lt=Kagura|Q1348260|WD=Q1348260}} dedication, {{ill|lt=Great Purification|Q11261910|WD=Q11261910|lt=Chino Ring}}, and bean-throwing will be held. *February 17th Prayer Festival * April 18th {{ill|lt=Regular Festival|Q11385469|WD=Q11385469}} ** Asashi Matsuri - After the regular festival ritual, the festival is carried out from the main shrine to Amaterasu Shrine across the street. |access-date=2022-07-13 |publisher=Susa Shrine Official}}. * April 19 Koden Festival ** Ryoou-mai - Performed following the Koden Festival ritual. {{Cite web|Japanese book|url=https://www.susa-jinja.jp/event/27/29 |title=Ryoou no Mai |access-date=2022-07-13 |publisher=Susa Shrine Official}}. It is thought that it was originally danced by a divine being of the Onmyoji type, but was transformed into one.** Hundred-handed Shinji - Archery ritual held in the afternoon to pray for the exorcism of demons and a good harvest. * June 30th {{ill|lt=Great Purification|Q11261910|WD=Q11261910}} * August 15 Kirimei Shinto ritual ** {{ill|lt=Nenbutsu Dance|Q11491438|WD=Q11491438}} - Held in the afternoon. Two ritual flowers are erected in the shrine grounds, and beneath them dancers wearing kimono (kimono) form a circle, chanting ``Naamamido'' and dancing monotonously to the beat of the flute. It is a dance with strong {{ill|lt=Shinbutsu syncretism|Q2279088|WD=Q2279088}} color, which is thought to have been influenced by Nembutsu saints in the {{ill|lt=Dengaku|Q3022455|WD=Q3022455}} style of dance in the medieval period. * October 17th Autumn Festival * November 23rd Niinamatsuri * December 31st Purification * 15th of every month {{ill|lt=Monthly Festival|Q11516161|WD=Q11516161}} === Cultural assets === === National Important Cultural Property === * Hyogo Kunishitei (unmarked contents) - {{ill|lt=1912|Q2077|WD=Q2077}} ({{ill|lt=Taisho|Q329174|WD=Q329174}} first year) Designated on September 3rd{{Cite web|Japanese book|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/6331 |title=Hyogo Kunishitei (unwritten contents) / National Treasures and Important Cultural Properties (Artworks) |access-date=2022-07-13 |publisher=Nationally Designated Cultural Properties Database / Agency for Cultural Affairs}}. {{ill|lt=Tenbun (era name)|Q1192853|WD=Q1192853|lt=Tenbun}} dedicated by {{ill|lt=Haruhisa Amago|Q3273106|WD=Q3273106}} in 23 ({{ill|lt=1554|Q6570|WD=Q6570}}). === Designated by Shimane Prefecture === ; Tangible cultural property * (Building) Main Hall of Susa Shrine - Designated on May 31, {{ill|lt=1966|Q2649|WD=Q2649}} ({{ill|lt=Showa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}}41). * (Sculpture) Bugaku mask "Nosori" - {{ill|lt=1972|Q2476|WD=Q2476}} (Designated on July 28, 1972) |access-date=2022-07-13 |publisher=Shimane Prefecture Education Agency Cultural Properties Division}}. * (Craft) Kurowei Armor Remains - {{ill|lt=2005|Q2019|WD=Q2019}} ({{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510}}17) Designated April 15th {{Cite web|Japanese book|url=https://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shimane/kougeihin.html |title=List of prefecture-designated cultural properties (crafts) / Cultural properties |access-date=2022-07-13 |publisher=Shimane Prefecture Education Agency Cultural Properties Division}}. ; Intangible folk cultural assets* Nenbutsu dance at Susa Shrine - {{ill|lt=1961|Q3696|WD=Q3696}} (Designated on June 13, 1966) |access-date=2022-07-13 |publisher=Shimane Prefecture Education Agency Cultural Properties Division}}. === Transportation === * From {{ill|lt=Izumo City Station|Q860288|WD=Q860288}}, take the {{ill|lt=Ichibata Bus|Q11352955|WD=Q11352955}} bound for Izumo Susa and get off at Susa bus stop. 3 km walk. * If you are using a car, there are over 10 parking spaces next to the temple grounds. === Footnotes === {{Footnote help}} === Notes === {{Notelist2}} === Source === {{Reflist|2}} === References === * Edited by {{ill|lt=Kenichi Tanigawa|Q2392596|WD=Q2392596}} "Japanese Gods, Shrines and Sacred Places 7 San'in" (newly reprinted) 2000 {{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|WD=Q4346470}} ISBN 978-4-560-02507-9 === Related items === {{Commonscat|Susa-jinja (Izumo)}} * {{ill|lt=Suga Shrine|Q11664579|WD=Q11664579}} ({{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751}}{{ill|lt=Unnan City|Q877935|WD=Q877935}}Daito Town) * {{ill|lt=Hii Shrine|Q11500716|WD=Q11500716}} (Kitsugu-cho, Unnan City, Shimane Prefecture) * {{ill|lt=Susa Shrine|Q11664499|WD=Q11664499}} (distribution of shrines with the same name) * {{ill|lt=Sada Town|Q7397459|WD=Q7397459}} * {{ill||lt=Yukarikan|UNKNOWN}} === External link === * [http://www.susa-jinja.jp/index.html Susa Shrine] -
